If false, they are placed starting from the end of the new array. fullLength int The final desired length. Returns T[] A new array of length fullLength with the original elements in the specified arrangement. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if fullLength is negative. Copy<T>(T[], int, int) Copies a portion of the array array (from start over length elements) into a new array. public static T[] Copy<T>(this T[] array, int start, int length) Parameters array T[] The source array. start int The starting index of the slice. length int The number of elements to copy. Mid<T>(T[], int) Retrieves a slice (sub-part) of this array starting at the specified start index to the end. public static T[] Mid<T>(this T[] s, int start) Parameters s T[] The source array from which to extract the slice. start int The starting index. Can be negative, in which case it counts from the end of the array. For example, -1 would refer to the last element. Returns T[] A new array containing elements from start to the end. Returns an empty array if start is out of range. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if s is null. Mid<T>(T[], int, int) Retrieves a slice (sub-part) of this array. Can be negative, in which case it counts from the end of the array. For example, -1 would refer to the last element. length int The number of elements to extract. Returns T[] A new array containing the requested slice. If start is out of range, this may return Empty<T>(). Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if s is null. PadLeft<T>(T[], int, T) Resizes the array to have length length, padding on the left with value if necessary. public static T[] PadLeft<T>(this T[] array, int length, T value = default) Parameters array T[] The source array. length int The new length of the array. value T The padding value to insert on the left if the array is enlarged. Returns T[] A new array of size length, padded on the right as needed. Type Parameters T Type of elements in the array. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if array is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown if length is smaller than the current array size. Replace<T>(T[], T[], T[]) Replaces non-overlapping occurrences of the subsequence toReplace in array with the subsequence replacement. public static T[] Replace<T>(this T[] array, T[] toReplace, T[] replacement) where T : IEquatable<T> Parameters array T[] The source array in which to perform replacements. toReplace T[] The subsequence to find and replace. replacement T[] The subsequence to substitute in place of toReplace. Returns T[] A new array with the replacements applied where the pattern is matched. Type Parameters T Type of elements, must implement IEquatable<T> for equality checks. Remarks Overlapping matches are not detected. Only distinct, non-overlapping occurrences are replaced. If toReplace is empty, no replacement is done and the original array is returned as-is. Replace<T>(IEnumerable<T>, T[], T[]) Scans the source for occurrences of the subsequence toReplace, and replaces them with the subsequence replacement. A naive, non-overlapping approach is used. public static IEnumerable<T> Replace<T>(this IEnumerable<T> source, T[] toReplace, T[] replacement) where T : IEquatable<T> Parameters source IEnumerable<T> The source sequence in which to perform replacements. toReplace T[] The subsequence to find and replace. replacement T[] The subsequence to substitute in place of toReplace. Remarks Overlapping matches are not recognized. For example, if toReplace is [1, 2, 1], and the source is [1, 2, 1, 2, 1], this method only replaces the first match, ignoring the second (overlapping) match. InnerJoin = 0 Returns only elements that appear in both sequences. LeftJoin = 1 Includes all elements from the left sequence, pairing them with matching right elements when available. RightJoin = 2 Includes all elements from the right sequence, pairing them with matching left elements when available.

Enumerating the cache (directly, or through Keys or Values) takes a point-in-time snapshot under that lock rather than returning a live cursor, so it never throws because of a concurrent modification and never observes a torn state — it just won't reflect mutations made after the snapshot was taken. As with most thread-safe collections, individual members are atomic but compound operations spanning more than one call (e.g. "add if not already present", or "read-modify-write") are not currently supported atomically by this type; there is no public way to make such a sequence atomic from outside the class. Property Value V The value associated with the specified key. If the cache exceeds its capacity, it removes the least recently used item. public void Add(K key, V value) Parameters key K The key of the element to add. value V The value of the element to add. api/Utils.Collections.html.title: Namespace Utils.Collections | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Collections.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Collections Classes CachedLoader<TKey, TValue> A class that provides a caching mechanism for loading resources. If a resource is not already loaded, it will be loaded and cached upon first access. DictionaryExtensions Provides thread-safe helper methods for working with Dictionary<TKey, TValue> instances. DictionaryMap<K, V> Implementation of IDictionaryMap<K, V> that wraps custom handlers for dictionary operations. DoubleIndexedDictionary<T1, T2> Represents a dictionary that maintains two-way mappings between keys of type T1 and values of type T2. This allows bidirectional access by both key and value. EnumerableComparer<T> Compares two sequences of values of comparable types. Supports custom comparers and optimizations for IReadOnlyList<T> and Span<T> where applicable. EnumerableEqualityComparer<T> A comparer for enumerables that allows comparison of sequences based on custom or default equality logic for each element. If both sequences implement IReadOnlyList<T>, the comparison first checks Count for an early exit. Uses Span<T> when applicable for performance. EnumerableEx Extension methods for working with IEnumerable<T> and related collections. ForwardFusion<T1, T2> Merges two sorted IEnumerable<T> collections based on the specified join type. The left list is considered the primary list in a left join, and vice versa for a right join. IndexedList<K, V> Indexed list. LRUCache<K, V> A Least Recently Used (LRU) cache implementation. This cache evicts the least recently accessed items when it reaches its capacity. MappedDictionary<K, V> A dictionary that uses a custom mapping for its operations. It allows customization of how the dictionary handles additions, removals, and value retrievals. ReadOnlyDictionaryMap<K, V> Default implementation of IReadOnlyDictionaryMap<K, V> that relies on delegate accessors. ReadOnlyMappedDictionary<K, V> List whose source is defined by accessors. SkipListDictionary<K, V> A dictionary whose keys are kept in sorted order using a SkipList<T> as the underlying data structure. All keyed operations run in O(log n) time on average. Iteration yields entries in ascending key order. SkipList<T> Represents a skip list, which is a probabilistic data structure that allows for fast search, insertion, and deletion operations. Structs Pack<T> Represents a value and its consecutive repetition count (used in packing). Interfaces IDictionaryMap<K, V> Interface representing a modifiable map that supports dictionary operations. IReadOnlyDictionaryMap<K, V> Defines accessor methods required to expose dictionary data without granting mutation capabilities. Enums JoinType Specifies the type of join operation. Delegates CachedLoader<TKey, TValue>.TryLoadValueDelegate Represents a method that attempts to load a value associated with the specified key.

api/Utils.Data.Sql.html.title: Namespace Utils.Data.Sql | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Data.Sql.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Data.Sql Classes CteDefinition Represents a CTE definition inside a WITH clause. DeletePart Represents a DELETE clause referencing the target to remove rows from. FromPart Represents the FROM clause of a SQL statement. GroupByPart Represents the GROUP BY clause of a SQL statement. HavingPart Represents the HAVING clause of a SQL statement. InsertPart Represents an INSERT clause identifying the target of the operation. IntoPart Represents the INTO clause of a SQL statement. LimitPart Represents the LIMIT clause of a SQL statement. OffsetPart Represents the OFFSET clause of a SQL statement. OrderByPart Represents the ORDER BY clause of a SQL statement. SelectPart Represents the SELECT clause of a SQL statement. SqlDeleteStatement Represents a parsed DELETE statement. SqlFormattingOptions Provides formatting configuration for SQL reconstruction. SqlInsertStatement Represents a parsed INSERT statement. SqlParseException Represents errors that occur during SQL parsing. SqlQuery Represents the root of an analysed SQL statement. SqlQueryAnalyzer Provides facilities to analyse SQL statements and reconstruct them from an abstract representation. SqlSegment Represents a section of a SQL statement, such as SELECT columns or a WHERE clause. SqlSelectStatement Represents a parsed SELECT statement. SqlStatement Represents a parsed SQL statement. SqlStatementPart Represents a typed part of a SQL statement built from a parsed segment. SqlSyntaxOptions Provides configuration options controlling how SQL identifiers and parameters are interpreted. SqlUpdateStatement Represents a parsed UPDATE statement. TailPart Represents trailing set operator content such as UNION clauses. UpdatePart Represents an UPDATE clause referencing the target to modify rows in. ValuesPart Represents the VALUES clause of a SQL statement. WherePart Represents the WHERE clause of a SQL statement. WithClause Represents a WITH clause containing CTE definitions. Enums SqlFormattingMode Determines how SQL text is formatted when rebuilt from the analyser output.

api/Utils.DependencyInjection.html.title: Namespace Utils.DependencyInjection | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.DependencyInjection.html.summary: Namespace Utils.DependencyInjection Classes HandlerCaller Default implementation of IHandlerCaller using IServiceProvider. InjectableAttribute Marks an interface as available for dependency injection. Implementations of interfaces decorated with this attribute can be automatically registered in the service container. InjectableClassAttribute Base attribute for injectable classes specifying the registration domain. ScopedAttribute Indicates that a class should be registered with a scoped lifetime. ServiceConfigurationHelper Helper methods to configure services based on injectable attributes. SingletonAttribute Indicates that a class should be registered as a singleton service. StaticAutoAttribute Applies to an IServiceConfigurator implementation to trigger compile-time generation of service registrations. TransientAttribute Indicates that a class should be registered with a transient lifetime. Structs CheckError<E> Represents a validation error produced by a specific check. Interfaces ICheck<T, E> Validates messages before they are handled. IHandlerCaller Dispatches messages to their associated handlers. IHandler<T> Defines a message handler for a specific message type. IInjectable Marker interface for services that can be injected. IServiceConfigurator Configures dependency injection services for an application. Implementations can customize registrations for a given IServiceCollection.

This allows callers to operate in normalized or physical spaces without manually performing the conversions required by the underlying IImageAccessor<T> implementation. All conversions clamp to the image bounds when delegated to Draw, ensuring consistency with the low-level routines. Constructors DrawF(IImageAccessor<T>) Initializes a new instance of the DrawF<T> class covering the entire image. public DrawF(IImageAccessor<T> imageAccessor) Parameters imageAccessor IImageAccessor<T> Accessor used to manipulate the underlying image. Remarks The viewport matches the full image bounds so one drawing unit equals one pixel. DrawF(IImageAccessor<T>, float, float, float, float) Initializes a new instance of the DrawF<T> class using a custom viewport. public DrawF(IImageAccessor<T> imageAccessor, float top, float left, float right, float down) Parameters imageAccessor IImageAccessor<T> Accessor used to manipulate the underlying image. top float Top boundary of the viewport. left float Left boundary of the viewport. right float Right boundary of the viewport. down float Bottom boundary of the viewport. Remarks The viewport boundaries define the coordinate space exposed to callers; the constructor computes the scaling factors that map the viewport to pixel positions. Properties Down Gets the bottom coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Down { get; } Property Value float Draw Gets the integer-based drawing helper that ultimately performs the rasterization work. public DrawI<T> Draw { get; } Property Value DrawI<T> Left Gets the left coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Left { get; } Property Value float Right Gets the right coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Right { get; } Property Value float Top Gets the top coordinate of the drawing viewport in the caller-provided space. public float Top { get; } Property Value float Methods ComputePixelPosition(PointF) Computes the integer pixel corresponding to the provided floating-point coordinates. public Point ComputePixelPosition(PointF p) Parameters p PointF Coordinates expressed in the viewport space. Returns Point The integer pixel location within the image. Remarks The conversion applies the viewport scaling and truncates the result to the nearest lower integer, matching the behavior of Floor(double) for positive coordinates. ComputePixelPosition(float, float) Computes the integer pixel corresponding to the provided floating-point coordinates. public Point ComputePixelPosition(float x, float y) Parameters x float Horizontal coordinate in the viewport space. y float Vertical coordinate in the viewport space. Returns Point The integer pixel location within the image. Remarks Values falling outside the viewport range result in negative or oversized pixel indices; the downstream Draw helper clamps them to the image dimensions when actually drawing. ComputePixelPositionF(PointF) Computes the floating-point pixel location corresponding to the provided viewport coordinates. public PointF ComputePixelPositionF(PointF p) Parameters p PointF Coordinates expressed in the viewport space. Returns PointF The floating-point pixel position inside the image. Remarks The method preserves the fractional part, which is useful for anti-aliasing or sub-pixel sampling scenarios. ComputePixelPositionF(float, float) Computes the floating-point pixel location corresponding to the provided viewport coordinates. public PointF ComputePixelPositionF(float x, float y) Parameters x float Horizontal coordinate in the viewport space. y float Vertical coordinate in the viewport space. Returns PointF The floating-point pixel position inside the image. Remarks Unlike ComputePixelPosition(float, float), this overload returns sub-pixel precision so callers can implement their own interpolation strategies. ComputePoint(PointF) Converts a pixel coordinate to the viewport space. public PointF ComputePoint(PointF p) Parameters p PointF Pixel coordinate. Returns PointF The corresponding viewport coordinate. ComputePoint(float, float) Converts a pixel coordinate to the viewport space. public PointF ComputePoint(float x, float y) Parameters x float Pixel horizontal coordinate. y float Pixel vertical coordinate. Returns PointF The corresponding viewport coordinate. Remarks The accessor reference is stored for the whole lifetime of the helper so that callers can perform several drawing operations without recreating the wrapper. DrawShapeThick(Func<float, float, T>, float, JoinStyle, IEnumerable<IDrawable>) Draws one or more shapes with a given stroke width and join style, using a color function that receives the arc length from the start and the signed perpendicular distance from the centre line. public void DrawShapeThick(Func<float, float, T> color, float width, JoinStyle join, IEnumerable<IDrawable> drawables) Parameters color Func<float, float, T> Delegate (arcLength, perpDist) → T: arcLength — accumulated path length from the start of the shape to the current pixel, in pixels. perpDist — signed perpendicular distance from the centre line, in pixels. Miter = 0 The outer edges are extended to their intersection point (miter). Falls back to Bevel when the resulting miter length exceeds miterLimit × halfWidth. Round = 2 The outer corner is rounded with a circular arc of radius equal to half the stroke width, centred on the join point.

api/Utils.Drawing.html.title: Namespace Utils.Drawing | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Drawing.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Drawing Classes BaseDrawing<T> Provides a base implementation that gives access to an image accessor for derived drawing helpers. Bezier Represents a Bézier curve that can be rasterized to points or line segments. Circle Represents an ellipse or circle drawable approximation that can be converted into points or segments. DrawF<T> Provides floating-point drawing helpers on top of DrawI<T>. DrawI<T> Provides integer-based drawing helpers for image accessors. DrawPoint Represents a sampled point produced by a drawing algorithm. MapBrush<T> Represents a brush that caches generated points to avoid drawing duplicates. Path Represents a drawable path composed of straight and Bézier segments. Pathes Represents a simple collection of drawable paths. Polygon Represents a closed polygon composed of connected segments. Polylines Represents a sequence of connected segments that are not necessarily closed. Segment Represents a straight line segment and exposes helper information used by raster drawing routines. Interfaces IBrush<T> Defines the contract for drawing brushes. IDrawable Describes an object that can be converted into drawing primitives such as segments or oriented points. Enums JoinStyle Determines how two stroke segments are joined at their common endpoint. Delegates DrawingColorMap<T> Represents a function that maps a drawing position and offset to a color value. UVMap<T> Represents a function that maps two-dimensional UV coordinates to a color value.

Returns ExpressionCompilerContext A populated context. Set(string, object?) Adds or replaces a symbol in the context. public void Set(string name, object? value) Parameters name string Symbol name. value object Symbol value. TryGet(string, out object?) Tries to resolve a symbol by name. public bool TryGet(string name, out object? value) Parameters name string Symbol name. value object Resolved value. Returns bool true when found; otherwise false. TryGetMember(GetMemberBinder, out object?) Provides the implementation for operations that get member values. Classes derived from the DynamicObject class can override this method to specify dynamic behavior for operations such as getting a value for a property. public override bool TryGetMember(GetMemberBinder binder, out object? result) Parameters binder GetMemberBinder Provides information about the object that called the dynamic operation. The binder.Name property provides the name of the member on which the dynamic operation is performed. For example, for the Console.WriteLine(sampleObject.SampleProperty) statement, where sampleObject is an instance of the class derived from the DynamicObject class, binder.Name returns "SampleProperty". The binder.IgnoreCase property specifies whether the member name is case-sensitive. result object The result of the get operation. For example, if the method is called for a property, you can assign the property value to result. Returns bool true if the operation is successful; otherwise, false. If this method returns false, the run-time binder of the language determines the behavior. (In most cases, a run-time exception is thrown.) TryInvokeMember(InvokeMemberBinder, object?[]?, out object?) Provides the implementation for operations that invoke a member. Classes derived from the DynamicObject class can override this method to specify dynamic behavior for operations such as calling a method. public override bool TryInvokeMember(InvokeMemberBinder binder, object?[]? args, out object? result) Parameters binder InvokeMemberBinder Provides information about the dynamic operation. The binder.Name property provides the name of the member on which the dynamic operation is performed. For example, for the statement sampleObject.SampleMethod(100), where sampleObject is an instance of the class derived from the DynamicObject class, binder.Name returns "SampleMethod". The binder.IgnoreCase property specifies whether the member name is case-sensitive. args object[] The arguments that are passed to the object member during the invoke operation. For example, for the statement sampleObject.SampleMethod(100), where sampleObject is derived from the DynamicObject class, args[0] is equal to 100. result object The result of the member invocation. Returns bool true if the operation is successful; otherwise, false. If this method returns false, the run-time binder of the language determines the behavior. (In most cases, a language-specific run-time exception is thrown.) TrySetMember(SetMemberBinder, object?) Provides the implementation for operations that set member values. Classes derived from the DynamicObject class can override this method to specify dynamic behavior for operations such as setting a value for a property. public override bool TrySetMember(SetMemberBinder binder, object? value) Parameters binder SetMemberBinder Provides information about the object that called the dynamic operation. The binder.Name property provides the name of the member to which the value is being assigned. For example, for the statement sampleObject.SampleProperty = "Test", where sampleObject is an instance of the class derived from the DynamicObject class, binder.Name returns "SampleProperty". The binder.IgnoreCase property specifies whether the member name is case-sensitive. value object The value to set to the member. For example, for sampleObject.SampleProperty = "Test", where sampleObject is an instance of the class derived from the DynamicObject class, the value is "Test". Returns bool true if the operation is successful; otherwise, false. If this method returns false, the run-time binder of the language determines the behavior. (In most cases, a language-specific run-time exception is thrown.) WriteToStream(Stream) Writes this context to a stream. public void WriteToStream(Stream stream) Parameters stream Stream Destination stream. Remarks Stream persistence serializes symbol names and values, including delegates and method groups.

api/Utils.Expressions.ExpressionSignatur.title: Class ExpressionSignatureAttribute | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Expressions.ExpressionSignatur.summary: Class ExpressionSignatureAttribute Namespace Utils.Expressions Assembly Utils.dll Marks a method or parameter as having a signature requirement for a certain ExpressionType. When used on a method, the method is considered for transformation if its attribute matches the current node type. When used on a parameter, it further restricts which sub-expressions are permissible. If certain ExpressionType values are not supported by this switch, they are simply returned as-is or an exception is thrown. protected static Expression CopyExpression(Expression e, params Expression[] parameters) Parameters e Expression The original expression to copy. parameters Expression[] The sub-expressions to insert into the copied expression. Returns Expression A new expression replicating the structure of e with possibly different sub-expressions. FinalizeExpression(Expression, Expression[]) Called if no custom transformation method (annotated with ExpressionSignatureAttribute) is found. Allows final post-processing. The default implementation throws an exception. protected virtual Expression FinalizeExpression(Expression e, Expression[] parameters) Parameters e Expression The expression being finalized. parameters Expression[] The sub-expressions or operands for e. Returns Expression A finalized expression. Exceptions Exception Thrown by default to indicate that transformation cannot be completed. PrepareExpression(Expression) Prepares an expression for transformation. Subclasses can override this to apply initial logic before the main Transform(Expression) switch (e.g., caching). The default implementation returns the expression unchanged. protected virtual Expression PrepareExpression(Expression e) Parameters e Expression The expression to prepare. Returns Expression The prepared expression. ReplaceArguments(Expression, ParameterExpression[], Expression[]) Replaces all occurrences of oldParameters within e with the corresponding items in newParameters. protected Expression ReplaceArguments(Expression e, ParameterExpression[] oldParameters, Expression[] newParameters) Parameters e Expression The expression in which parameter references are replaced. oldParameters ParameterExpression[] The parameters to remove. newParameters Expression[] The new expressions that replace oldParameters. Returns Expression A copy of e where specified parameters are replaced. Transform(Expression) Applies transformation rules to a given expression, returning a (potentially) modified expression. This method checks for known signatures (via ExpressionSignatureAttribute-annotated methods) and if a match is found, invokes the corresponding transformation function. If no signature method matches, it calls FinalizeExpression(Expression, Expression[]) by default. protected Expression Transform(Expression e) Parameters e Expression The expression to transform. Returns Expression A possibly rewritten expression.

GetStaticPropertyOrField(Type, string) Retrieves a static property or field on type with the given name. public MemberInfo GetStaticPropertyOrField(Type type, string name) Parameters type Type The Type to inspect for the property or field. name string The name of the desired member. ResolveType(string) Attempts to resolve a type by its name. public Type ResolveType(string name) Parameters name string The name of the type to resolve, potentially including a namespace. Returns Type A Type object if resolution is successful; otherwise null. ResolveType(string, Type[]) Attempts to resolve a generic type by its name, using the provided generic parameters. public Type ResolveType(string name, Type[] genericParameters) Parameters name string The name of the type to resolve. genericParameters Type[] An array of types to use as generic arguments. Returns Type A constructed Type object if resolution is successful; otherwise null. SelectConstructor(IEnumerable<ConstructorInfo>, Expression[]) Selects the most suitable constructor from the provided constructors based on the specified arguments. This method uses parameter matching and assigns a "distance" score to find the best match. public (ConstructorInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? SelectConstructor(IEnumerable<ConstructorInfo> constructors, Expression[] arguments) Parameters constructors IEnumerable<ConstructorInfo> A sequence of ConstructorInfo candidates. arguments Expression[] The argument expressions used in the constructor call. Returns (ConstructorInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? A tuple containing the ConstructorInfo and the adjusted Expression array, or null if no suitable constructor is found. SelectMethod(IEnumerable<MethodInfo>, Expression, Type[], Expression[]) Selects the most suitable method from the provided methods, optionally applying genericParameters if it's a generic method definition. Determines a "distance" score to pick the best match, then returns the adjusted expressions. public (MethodInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? SelectMethod(IEnumerable<MethodInfo> methods, Expression obj, Type[] genericParameters, Expression[] arguments) Parameters methods IEnumerable<MethodInfo> A sequence of MethodInfo candidates. obj Expression An Expression representing the target object (for instance methods), or null for static calls. genericParameters Type[] Optional array of types to use if the method is generic. arguments Expression[] The argument expressions for the method call. Returns (MethodInfo Method, Expression[] Parameters)? A tuple containing the MethodInfo and the adjusted Expression array, or null if no suitable method is found. TryGetConstant(string, out ConstantExpression) Attempts to retrieve a named constant from the internal Constants dictionary. public bool TryGetConstant(string name, out ConstantExpression constantExpression) Parameters name string The name of the constant. constantExpression ConstantExpression When this method returns, contains a ConstantExpression of the constant value if found. Returns bool true if the constant was found; otherwise false.

api/Utils.Expressions.VBSyntax.Runtime.h.summary: Namespace Utils.Expressions.VBSyntax.Runtime Classes VBSyntaxCompilerContext Represents a mutable symbol context used by VBSyntaxExpressionCompiler. Symbols can be plain objects, delegates, or expression nodes. VBSyntaxExpressionCompiler Compiles VB-like source text into LINQ expression trees. Supports arithmetic, logical and comparison operators, control flow (If/For/For Each/While/Do), variable declarations, and function definitions. VBSyntaxToken Represents a VB-like token produced by VBSyntaxTokenParser. VBSyntaxTokenParser Provides tokenization and parsing support for VB-like expressions using the Utils.Parser infrastructure.

api/Utils.Expressions.html.title: Namespace Utils.Expressions | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Expressions.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Expressions Classes BlockExpressionBuilder A helper class that builds expression blocks while minimizing the number of declared variables. CompileException Represents the base class for compile-time exceptions that occur during expression compilation in the Utils.Expressions namespace. ConstantNumericAttribute A specialized attribute that indicates the expected parameter is a ConstantExpression holding a numeric value, optionally restricted to a specific set of allowed numeric values. ExpressionCallSignatureAttribute Marks a method as matching only method-call expressions that invoke a specific function name on a specific type. This is a specialized ExpressionSignatureAttribute for calls. ExpressionCompilerContext Represents a dynamic symbol context shared by expression compilers. ExpressionEx Provides helper methods for building and manipulating expression trees. ExpressionOptimiser Provides a standard optimization pass over expression trees. ExpressionSignatureAttribute Marks a method or parameter as having a signature requirement for a certain ExpressionType. When used on a method, the method is considered for transformation if its attribute matches the current node type. When used on a parameter, it further restricts which sub-expressions are permissible. ExpressionTransformer Provides an abstract base class to transform or rewrite LINQ expression trees. Subclasses may override the transformation logic for specific expression signatures. ExpressionUtils Provides utility helpers for analysing and comparing expression trees. FindTypeException Thrown when a type cannot be found during a type resolution process. FormattedPart Represents a formatted part of an interpolated string, containing an expression and optional format/alignment specifications. InterpolatedStringParser Parses an interpolated string into its constituent parts, which can be either literals or formatted expressions. LiteralPart Represents a literal (non-interpolated) part of a string. Parenthesis Describes a set of parenthesis tokens used by the expression parser. ParseDuplicateParameterNameException Thrown when the parser encounters a parameter name that is already in use. ParseException Represents the base class for all parsing exceptions that occur during expression parsing in the Utils.Expressions namespace. ParseNoEndException Thrown when an expected closing symbol is not found in the parsed expression. ParseUnfindTypeException Thrown when a type name cannot be resolved or found by the parser. ParseUnknownException Thrown when an unknown symbol is encountered by the parser. ParseUnmatchException Thrown when a pair of opening and closing symbols do not match in the parsed expression. ParseWrongSymbolException Thrown when the parser encounters a symbol that is incorrect for the current context. ParserOptions Provides configuration settings and default mappings for parsing expressions, including operator priorities, numeric type levels, default type keywords, and numeric suffix mappings. ReturnTypeAttribute A specialized attribute that indicates the matched expression's return type must be assignable to a specified type. Useful for restricting the type of an operand beyond its ExpressionType. Interfaces IDistanceValue<T> Represents a pairing of a distance measure and a value, typically used for scoring or ranking operations in method or constructor selection. IExpressionCompiler Defines a lightweight contract for expression compilers that turn source text into LINQ expression trees. IInterpolatedStringPart Represents a part of an interpolated string, which can be either literal text or a formatted expression. IResolver Provides functionality to resolve types, constructors, methods, and members at runtime, as well as to manage constants in expressions. ITypeFinder Provides methods for finding types and extension methods at runtime.

api/Utils.Files.PathUtils.html.summary: Class PathUtils Namespace Utils.Files Assembly Utils.dll Provides helpers for enumerating directories and files when the path contains wildcard segments. Each public method expands intermediate directories before delegating the final segment to the requested enumeration API. public static class PathUtils Inheritance object PathUtils Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods EnumerateDirectories(DirectoryInfo, string) Enumerates only directories (as DirectoryInfo) beneath the specified baseDir, allowing wildcards in intermediate segments and in the final segment for directories. public static IEnumerable<DirectoryInfo> EnumerateDirectories(this DirectoryInfo baseDir, string path) Parameters baseDir DirectoryInfo path string Returns IEnumerable<DirectoryInfo> EnumerateDirectories(string) Enumerates only directories for the final segment, while managing wildcards in intermediate segments as directories. The final call is EnumerateDirectories(string, string) on each matched parent. public static IEnumerable<string> EnumerateDirectories(string path) Parameters path string A path that may contain multiple wildcard segments (e.g. "C:\Root\Sub*\Another*\EndDir??"). Returns IEnumerable<string> Fully qualified directory paths matching the final wildcard, after expanding any intermediate wildcard directories. EnumerateFileSystemEntries(DirectoryInfo, string) Enumerates both files and directories (as FileSystemInfo) beneath the specified baseDir, allowing wildcards in intermediate segments and in the final segment for either directories or files. public static IEnumerable<FileSystemInfo> EnumerateFileSystemEntries(this DirectoryInfo baseDir, string path) Parameters baseDir DirectoryInfo path string Returns IEnumerable<FileSystemInfo> EnumerateFileSystemEntries(string) Enumerates both files and directories for the final segment, while managing wildcards in intermediate segments as directories. The final call is EnumerateFileSystemEntries(string, string) on each matched parent. public static IEnumerable<string> EnumerateFileSystemEntries(string path) Parameters path string A path that may contain multiple wildcard segments (e.g. "C:\Root\Sub*\Another**.*"). Returns IEnumerable<string> Fully qualified paths (files or directories) matching the final wildcard, after expanding any intermediate wildcard directories. EnumerateFiles(DirectoryInfo, string) Enumerates only files (as FileInfo) beneath the specified baseDir, allowing wildcards in intermediate segments and in the final segment for files. public static IEnumerable<FileInfo> EnumerateFiles(this DirectoryInfo baseDir, string path) Parameters baseDir DirectoryInfo path string Returns IEnumerable<FileInfo> EnumerateFiles(string) Enumerates only files for the final segment, while managing wildcards in intermediate segments as directories. The final call is EnumerateFiles(string, string) on each matched parent. public static IEnumerable<string> EnumerateFiles(string path) Parameters path string A path that may contain multiple wildcard segments (e.g. "C:\Root\Sub*\Another*\File??.txt"). Returns IEnumerable<string> Fully qualified file paths matching the final wildcard, after expanding any intermediate wildcard directories.

All subsequent drawing commands (StartAt(float, float), LineTo(float, float), BezierTo(params (float x, float y)[]), ClosePath()) use coordinates relative to x and y, transformed by transform. void BeginDrawGlyph(float x, float y, Matrix3x2 transform) Parameters x float Absolute X position of the glyph origin in the target coordinate space. y float Absolute Y position of the glyph origin in the target coordinate space. transform Matrix3x2 Affine transformation applied to glyph-local coordinates (scale, rotation, shear). Use Identity when no transformation is needed. BezierTo(params (float x, float y)[]) Draws one or more Bézier curves from the current position using the given control points. void BezierTo(params (float x, float y)[] points) Parameters points (float x, float y)[] An array of control and end points for the curve. ClosePath() Closes the current subpath. An implicit straight line is drawn from the current point back to the starting point of the subpath, and the contour is marked as geometrically closed (equivalent to PostScript closepath, SVG Z, PDF h). void ClosePath() EndDrawGlyph() Signals the end of the current glyph rendering pass. Any per-glyph state set by the preceding BeginDrawGlyph(float, float, Matrix3x2) call is discarded. void EndDrawGlyph() LineTo(float, float) Draws a straight line from the current position to the specified point. void LineTo(float x, float y) Parameters x float Target X coordinate. y float Target Y coordinate. StartAt(float, float) Starts a new drawing path at the given position. void StartAt(float x, float y) Parameters x float X coordinate in drawing units. y float Y coordinate in drawing units.

Properties Count Gets the number of generated paths. public int Count { get; } Property Value int this[int] Gets the path at the specified index. public Path this[int index] { get; } Parameters index int Index of the desired path. Property Value Path Methods BeginDrawGlyph(float, float, Matrix3x2) Signals the beginning of a glyph rendering pass. All subsequent drawing commands (StartAt(float, float), LineTo(float, float), BezierTo(params (float x, float y)[]), ClosePath()) use coordinates relative to x and y, transformed by transform. public void BeginDrawGlyph(float x, float y, Matrix3x2 transform) Parameters x float Absolute X position of the glyph origin in the target coordinate space. y float Absolute Y position of the glyph origin in the target coordinate space. transform Matrix3x2 Affine transformation applied to glyph-local coordinates (scale, rotation, shear). Use Identity when no transformation is needed. BezierTo(params (float x, float y)[]) Draws one or more Bézier curves from the current position using the given control points. public void BezierTo(params (float x, float y)[] points) Parameters points (float x, float y)[] An array of control and end points for the curve. ClosePath() Closes the current subpath. An implicit straight line is drawn from the current point back to the starting point of the subpath, and the contour is marked as geometrically closed (equivalent to PostScript closepath, SVG Z, PDF h). public void ClosePath() EndDrawGlyph() Signals the end of the current glyph rendering pass. Any per-glyph state set by the preceding BeginDrawGlyph(float, float, Matrix3x2) call is discarded. public void EndDrawGlyph() GetEnumerator() Returns an enumerator that iterates through the collection. public IEnumerator<Path> GetEnumerator() Returns IEnumerator<Path> An enumerator that can be used to iterate through the collection. LineTo(float, float) Draws a straight line from the current position to the specified point. public void LineTo(float x, float y) Parameters x float Target X coordinate. y float Target Y coordinate. StartAt(float, float) Starts a new drawing path at the given position. public void StartAt(float x, float y) Parameters x float X coordinate in drawing units. y float Y coordinate in drawing units.

Returns IGlyph An instance of IGlyph representing the character. Remarks Glyphs are looked up by their character code treated as CID. Glyphs are stored internally by CID (int, which may exceed MaxValue), but this method takes a char -- a limitation of the IFont interface, not of this class -- so CIDs above 0xFFFF can never be reached through this API. GetSpacingCorrection(char, char) Returns the horizontal spacing correction between two adjacent characters. Returns CidKeyedFont A new CidKeyedFont. LoadPfb(Reader) Loads a CID-Keyed PostScript Type 1 PFB font using a Reader. Blocks are concatenated and converted to the ASCII representation consumed by the LoadPfa(Stream) method. public static CidKeyedFont LoadPfb(Reader reader) Parameters reader Reader Reader providing PFB data. api/Utils.Fonts.PostScript.PostScriptFon.summary: Class PostScriptFont Namespace Utils.Fonts.PostScript Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll Very small PostScript font loader based on a custom text representation. The intention is to mimic a subset of the Type 1 syntax without parsing the full PostScript language. Each glyph is declared as shown below: Glyph: A Width: 500 Height: 600 Baseline: 0 Path: M 0 0 L 10 0 ... EndGlyph For a description of the real Type 1 format see Adobe Technical Note 5040. public class PostScriptFont : IFont Inheritance object PostScriptFont Implements IFont Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) Typically 70 + ascent * Scale. public float BaseLineY { get; } Property Value float Scale Gets the scale factor mapping font units to pixels at a nominal 100-pixel cap height. Returns byte[] Decrypted byte array. GetGlyph(char) Returns the glyph associated with a given character. public IGlyph GetGlyph(char c) Parameters c char The character to retrieve the glyph for. Returns IGlyph An instance of IGlyph representing the character. Remarks Only glyphs defined in the input are available. GetSpacingCorrection(char, char) Returns the horizontal spacing correction between two adjacent characters. This can be used for kerning or other spacing adjustments. public float GetSpacingCorrection(char before, char after) Parameters before char The preceding character. after char The following character. Returns float A float value representing the spacing correction in font units. Remarks Kerning is not supported for this simple implementation. Load(Stream) Loads a font in the simplified text form used by this demo. This is not a full Type 1 parser but follows a similar structure for learning purposes. public static PostScriptFont Load(Stream stream) Parameters stream Stream Stream containing the custom font text. Returns PostScriptFont Parsed PostScriptFont instance. LoadPfa(Stream) Loads a PostScript Type 1 PFA font stream. The implementation only understands a tiny fraction of the format sufficient for the unit tests. See Adobe Technical Note 5040 for the real specification. public static PostScriptFont LoadPfa(Stream stream) Parameters stream Stream Input PFA stream. Returns PostScriptFont Instance of PostScriptFont. LoadPfb(Stream) Loads a binary Type 1 (PFB) font stream. public static PostScriptFont LoadPfb(Stream stream) Parameters stream Stream Input PFB stream. WritePfb(string, Writer) Writes ASCII Type 1 content to the binary PFB format using a Writer. public static void WritePfb(string ascii, Writer writer) Parameters ascii string Plain ASCII Type 1 data. writer Writer Destination binary writer.

Properties BaseLine Gets the baseline offset of the glyph. public float BaseLine { get; } Property Value float Height Gets the height of the glyph. public float Height { get; } Property Value float Width Gets the width of the glyph (advance width). public float Width { get; } Property Value float Methods ToGraphic(IGraphicConverter) Converts the glyph into graphic instructions using the given converter. public void ToGraphic(IGraphicConverter graphicConverter) Parameters graphicConverter IGraphicConverter An implementation to receive drawing instructions. api/Utils.Fonts.PostScript.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts.PostScript | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.PostScript.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts.PostScript Classes CidKeyedFont Very small loader for CID-Keyed Type 1 PostScript fonts. Only a subset of the format is implemented. Refer to Adobe Technical Note 5014 for the full specification. PostScriptFont Very small PostScript font loader based on a custom text representation. The intention is to mimic a subset of the Type 1 syntax without parsing the full PostScript language. Each glyph is declared as shown below: Glyph: A Width: 500 Height: 600 Baseline: 0 Path: M 0 0 L 10 0 ... EndGlyph For a description of the real Type 1 format see Adobe Technical Note 5040. PostScriptGlyph Represents a glyph defined by a sequence of basic PostScript path commands. The commands correspond loosely to the operators described in Adobe's Type 1 specification. Type3Font Minimal loader for PostScript Type 3 fonts. Only a tiny subset of the language is interpreted: setcachedevice for metrics and the basic path commands moveto, lineto, curveto and closepath. See Adobe Technical Note 5015 for the full details. Type42Font Minimal loader for PostScript Type 42 fonts which encapsulate a TrueType font. This implementation extracts the embedded TrueType data from the /sfnts array and delegates glyph retrieval to TrueTypeFont. For the official format see Adobe Technical Note 5012. Structs PostScriptGlyph.PathCommand Represents a single drawing command extracted from the font. The coordinates use the same units as the original charstring. Enums PostScriptGlyph.PathCommandType Enumeration of possible path command types supported by this simplified glyph representation.

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.HeadTable.html.title: Class HeadTable | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.HeadTable.html.summary: Class HeadTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'head' table contains global information about the font. It records facts such as the font version number, creation and modification dates, revision number, and basic typographic data that applies to the font as a whole. This includes the font bounding box, the primary writing direction, and other information regarding glyph placement within the em square. The checksum is used to verify the integrity of the font data and can help distinguish between similar fonts. ushort NoControlPoint Sentinel value in the control-point array indicating no control point for that slot. public const ushort NoControlPoint = 65535 Field Value ushort RomanBaseline Roman (Latin) baseline — most glyphs sit above, descenders extend below. public const ushort RomanBaseline = 0 Field Value ushort Properties ControlPoints Gets or sets the 32-element control-point index array (one UInt16 per baseline slot). Each element is a control point index within StandardGlyph, or NoControlPoint (0xFFFF) if that baseline has no control point. Applies to ControlPointNoMap and ControlPointWithMap. public ushort[] ControlPoints { get; set; } Property Value ushort[] DefaultBaseline Gets or sets the default baseline class (0–31) applied to all glyphs not covered by the per-glyph lookup (used by formats 1 and 3) or to all glyphs (formats 0 and 2). public ushort DefaultBaseline { get; set; } Property Value ushort Deltas Gets or sets the 32-element delta array (one Int16 FUnit value per baseline slot). Each element is the distance from the natural baseline of glyphs assigned to that baseline class to the baseline position itself. Applies to DistanceNoMap and DistanceWithMap. public short[] Deltas { get; set; } Property Value short[] Format Gets or sets the sub-table format. public BslnTable.BslnFormat Format { get; set; } Property Value BslnTable.BslnFormat GlyphBaselineMap Gets or sets the mapping from glyph index to baseline class (0–31). Glyphs absent from this map use DefaultBaseline. Applies to DistanceWithMap and ControlPointWithMap. Written as AAT lookup Format 6 (single table); all five common read formats are supported. public Dictionary<ushort, ushort> GlyphBaselineMap { get; set; } Property Value Dictionary<ushort, ushort> Length Gets the length, in bytes, of the table data. public override int Length { get; } Property Value int StandardGlyph Gets or sets the glyph index of the standard glyph whose post-hinting control points define all baseline positions. Applies to ControlPointNoMap and ControlPointWithMap. public ushort StandardGlyph { get; set; } Property Value ushort Version Gets or sets the table version (fixed32). Default 0x00010000. public int Version { get; set; } Property Value int Methods ReadData(Reader) Reads the table data from the specified reader. public override void ReadData(Reader data) Parameters data Reader The reader from which to read the table data. WriteData(Writer) Writes the table data to the specified writer. public override void WriteData(Writer data) Parameters data Writer The writer to which the table data is written.

Must not be null. intermedStartCoords short[] Intermediate start coordinates, or null. intermedEndCoords short[] Intermediate end coordinates, or null. pointNumbers int[] CVT indices, or null for all CVTs. deltas short[] Delta values to apply. Properties Deltas Gets or sets the delta values (signed 16-bit) to add to each targeted CVT entry. When PointNumbers is null, the array is indexed directly by CVT index; otherwise Deltas[i] applies to PointNumbers[i]. public short[] Deltas { get; set; } Property Value short[] IntermedEndCoords Gets the end coordinates of an intermediate region, or null if this is a non-intermediate region. Length must equal AxisCount when set. public short[] IntermedEndCoords { get; init; } Property Value short[] IntermedStartCoords Gets the start coordinates of an intermediate region, or null if this is a non-intermediate (peak-only) region. Length must equal AxisCount when set. public short[] IntermedStartCoords { get; init; } Property Value short[] PeakCoords Gets the peak coordinates of the variation region, one F2Dot14 value per design axis. F2Dot14 is a signed 2.14 fixed-point number: divide by 16 384 to get the real value (e.g. 0x4000 = 1.0, 0x0000 = 0.0, 0xC000 = –1.0). Length must equal AxisCount. public short[] PeakCoords { get; init; } Property Value short[] PointNumbers Gets or sets the CVT indices to which Deltas apply, or null to indicate that deltas apply to all CVT entries (in order). public int[] PointNumbers { get; set; } Property Value int[]

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.CvarTable.htm.summary: Class CvarTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The CVT variations table (tag: 'cvar') provides delta adjustments to CVT values for different instances of a variable font. It uses the tuple variation store format: each tuple variation covers one region of the variation space and carries packed deltas for the CVT entries it affects. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.DsigTable.htm.summary: Class DsigTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'DSIG' (Digital Signature) table contains one or more cryptographic signatures that attest to the authenticity and integrity of a font. Most tools treat this table as an opaque blob: they preserve it on read and discard or regenerate it on write. This implementation stores the entire table as raw bytes after the 8-byte outer header. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.GlyfTable.htm.summary: Class GlyfTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'glyf' table contains the data that defines the appearance of the glyphs in the font. It includes the specification of the points that describe the contours that make up each glyph outline as well as the instructions for grid-fitting the glyph. The table supports both simple glyphs and compound glyphs (i.e. glyphs composed of other glyphs). api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LtshTable.htm.title: Class LtshTable | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.LtshTable.htm.summary: Class LtshTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'LTSH' (Linear Threshold) table stores a per-glyph pixel-per-em threshold value. Below this threshold the glyph is non-linear (i.e. hinting is used); at or above it the glyph scales linearly and hinting can be suppressed for better appearance. A value of 1 means the glyph is always linear; 0 means the threshold is unknown. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.VmtxTable.htm.title: Class VmtxTable | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.VmtxTable.htm.summary: Class VmtxTable Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll The 'vmtx' table contains metric information for the vertical layout of each glyph in the font. It is the vertical analogue of the 'hmtx' table. Its layout consists of a series of longVerMetric records (advance height + top side bearing) followed by additional top-side-bearing-only values for the remaining glyphs. The number of long-metric entries is given by NumOfLongVerMetrics, and the total glyph count by NumGlyphs. api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF.Tables Classes AcntTable The accent attachment table (tag: 'acnt') provides a space-efficient method of combining component glyphs into compound glyphs to form accents. Accented glyphs are a very restricted subclass of compound glyphs. AcntTable.AccentDescription Represents the description of an accented glyph, associating a primary glyph with one or more accent attachments. AcntTable.AccentDescription.Multiple Format 1 description: two or more secondary components referenced via the extension sub-table. Stored as 6 bytes: UInt16 primary word (bit15=1), Int32 absolute extension offset. AcntTable.AccentDescription.Single Format 0 description: a single secondary (accent) component identified by a direct table index. Stored as 4 bytes: UInt16 primary word, byte attachment point, byte secondary index. AvarTable The 'avar' (Axis Variations) table maps normalized design-axis coordinates through per-axis piecewise-linear segment maps, allowing non-linear axis progressions and ensuring that certain canonical normalized coordinates (−1, 0, +1) map to themselves. AvarTable.SegmentMap The piecewise-linear segment map for one design axis. Must include mappings for –1→–1, 0→0 and +1→+1 as required by the spec. BslnTable The baseline table (tag: 'bsln') defines the positions of baselines for each script in the font, allowing mixed-script text to align glyphs from different writing systems on a common line. CmapTable Represents the 'cmap' table which provides the character-to-glyph mapping for a TrueType font. The table contains one or more subtables (each identified by a platform ID and platform-specific ID) that define different mapping formats. CmapTable.CmapSubtable Represents a subtable identifier defined by a platform ID and platform-specific ID. Used as a key for the cmap subtables. CvarTable The CVT variations table (tag: 'cvar') provides delta adjustments to CVT values for different instances of a variable font. It uses the tuple variation store format: each tuple variation covers one region of the variation space and carries packed deltas for the CVT entries it affects. CvarTable.TupleVariation One tuple variation record: covers one region of the variation space and carries the CVT deltas to apply when a font instance falls inside that region. CvtTable The CVT (Control Value Table) contains a series of signed 16-bit integers that are used as control values during the hinting process. These values are used by the font program to adjust glyph outlines. DsigTable The 'DSIG' (Digital Signature) table contains one or more cryptographic signatures that attest to the authenticity and integrity of a font. Most tools treat this table as an opaque blob: they preserve it on read and discard or regenerate it on write. This implementation stores the entire table as raw bytes after the 8-byte outer header. FdscTable The 'fdsc' (Font Descriptors) table provides a set of named scalar values that describe characteristics of the font — such as optical weight, contrast, or size. Each descriptor has a 4-byte tag and a 16.16 fixed-point value. FdscTable.FontDescriptor One named descriptor entry: a 4-byte tag and a 16.16 fixed-point scalar value. FeatTable The 'feat' (Feature Name) table lists the Apple Advanced Typography (AAT) layout features supported by the font and their available settings. Each feature has a numeric identifier, a name-table entry, and one or more named setting values. FeatTable.FeatureNameRecord Describes one AAT layout feature, its flags, its name-table entry, and its available settings. FeatTable.FeatureSetting One named setting for a feature (e.g. "On", "Off", "Standard Ligatures"). FmtxTable The 'fmtx' (Font Metrics) table provides glyph-based typographic metrics: it identifies a reference glyph whose phantom points carry the horizontal and vertical metric data, and stores the indices of those phantom points within the glyph. FpgmTable The FPGM (Font Program) table contains a set of instructions (bytecode) that are executed once when the font is loaded. These instructions establish routines used for hinting glyphs. FvarTable The 'fvar' (Font Variations) table defines the design axes and named instances for a variable font. Each axis specifies a minimum, default and maximum design-space coordinate, and each named instance represents a specific position in the design space (e.g. "Regular", "Bold", "Light"). FvarTable.InstanceRecord Represents one named instance (a specific point in the design space). FvarTable.VariationAxisRecord Describes one design axis of the variable font. GaspTable The 'gasp' table contains information which describes the preferred rasterization techniques for the typeface when it is rendered on grayscale-capable devices at various sizes. Each entry specifies a maximum pixels-per-em threshold and the corresponding rendering behaviour. GaspTable.GaspRange Describes the recommended rendering behaviour for one PPEM size range. GlyfTable The 'glyf' table contains the data that defines the appearance of the glyphs in the font. It includes the specification of the points that describe the contours that make up each glyph outline as well as the instructions for grid-fitting the glyph. The table supports both simple glyphs and compound glyphs (i.e. glyphs composed of other glyphs). HdmxTable The 'hdmx' (Horizontal Device Metrics) table stores pre-computed advance widths for specific pixels-per-em sizes, allowing rasterizers to avoid recomputing them at render time. Each HdmxTable.DeviceRecord covers one PPEM size and contains one advance-width byte per glyph. HdmxTable.DeviceRecord Contains per-glyph advance widths (in pixels) for one specific PPEM size. HheaTable The 'hhea' table contains information needed to layout fonts whose characters are written horizontally, that is, either left-to-right or right-to-left. This table provides global metrics for the font, including typographic values such as ascent, descent, line gap, and other essential data used during glyph layout. HmtxTable The 'hmtx' table contains metric information for the horizontal layout of each glyph in the font. It begins with the hMetrics array, where each entry consists of the advance width and left side bearing for a glyph. The number of long horizontal metrics is specified in the 'hhea' table, and in a monospaced font, only one entry is required (but must still be present). KernTable Represents the 'kern' table which contains kerning pairs for adjusting the spacing between glyphs in a TrueType font. This implementation supports the most common format (version 0) with a single horizontal kerning subtable. LcarTable The 'lcar' (Ligature Caret) table specifies caret positions within ligature glyphs so that a text caret can be placed at intermediate positions inside a ligature (e.g. between the two characters of a fi ligature). Each ligature glyph has one or more caret values. LocaTable The 'loca' table stores the offsets to the locations of the glyphs in the font relative to the beginning of the 'glyf' table. Its purpose is to provide quick access to the data for a particular glyph. For example, in the standard Macintosh glyph ordering, the character A is the 76th glyph in a font. The 'loca' table stores the offset from the start of the 'glyf' table to the position at which the data for each glyph can be found. LtshTable The 'LTSH' (Linear Threshold) table stores a per-glyph pixel-per-em threshold value. Below this threshold the glyph is non-linear (i.e. hinting is used); at or above it the glyph scales linearly and hinting can be suppressed for better appearance. A value of 1 means the glyph is always linear; 0 means the threshold is unknown. MaxpTable The 'maxp' table establishes the memory requirements for a font. It begins with a table version number and the number of glyphs in the font. The remaining entries set maximum values for various parameters (such as maximum points, contours, and storage), which are used to allocate resources for the font. OpbdTable The 'opbd' (Optical Bounds) table provides per-glyph adjustments that shift the effective optical edge of a glyph inward or outward, improving the visual alignment of text at the margins. Each glyph can have four adjustments: left, top, right, and bottom. Os2Table The 'OS/2' table contains metrics and other data required for correct rendering on Windows and OS/2 platforms. It is present in virtually every TrueType/OpenType font and describes properties such as weight, width, style, typographic metrics, Unicode character ranges and vendor identification. PcltTable The 'PCLT' table contains PCL 5 characterization data: information needed to identify and render the font on PCL 5-capable printers. The table has a fixed size of 54 bytes. PostTable The 'post' table contains information needed to use a TrueType font on a PostScript printer. It provides data for the FontInfo dictionary, the PostScript names for all glyphs, and memory usage information for the PostScript driver. PrepTable The PREP (Control Value Program) table contains instructions (bytecode) that are executed before rendering any glyph. These instructions typically perform scaling and other pre-processing needed for proper hinting. PropTable The 'prop' (Glyph Properties) table assigns directional and typographic property bits to each glyph. Properties include writing direction (LTR/RTL/neutral), hanging punctuation, kerning suppression, and attachment class. TrakTable The 'trak' (Tracking) table specifies inter-character spacing adjustments as a function of point size. For each nominal tracking level (e.g. "loose", "normal", "tight") a set of per-size values (in font design units) gives the amount to add to each glyph's advance width. TrakTable.TrackData One direction's worth of tracking data (horizontal or vertical). TrakTable.TrackEntry One entry in the tracking table: a nominal tracking value and a name-table entry, with per-size spacing adjustments. VheaTable The 'vhea' table contains information needed to lay out fonts whose characters are written vertically, that is, either top-to-bottom or bottom-to-top. It is the vertical analogue of the 'hhea' (horizontal header) table and has the same 36-byte fixed structure. VmtxTable The 'vmtx' table contains metric information for the vertical layout of each glyph in the font. It is the vertical analogue of the 'hmtx' table. Its layout consists of a series of longVerMetric records (advance height + top side bearing) followed by additional top-side-bearing-only values for the remaining glyphs. The number of long-metric entries is given by NumOfLongVerMetrics, and the total glyph count by NumGlyphs. Structs AcntTable.ExtensionEntry A single entry in the extension sub-table (2 bytes), referencing one secondary glyph attachment. Bit layout: [bit15=isLast | bits14-8=SecondaryInfoIndex(7) | bits7-0=PrimaryAttachmentPoint]. AcntTable.SecondaryEntry A single entry in the secondary (accent) data table (3 bytes): Int16 secondary glyph index followed by one byte attachment point. AvarTable.AxisValueMap A single input→output coordinate mapping entry within a AvarTable.SegmentMap. Both values are F2Dot14 (divide by 16 384 to obtain the real normalized value). OpbdTable.OpticalBounds Optical bound adjustments for one glyph (left, top, right, bottom), either in design units (format 0) or contour-point indices (format 1). Enums BslnTable.BslnFormat Discriminates the four bsln sub-table layouts. GaspTable.GaspBehavior Rasterization behaviour flags for a gasp range. Version 0 supports only Gridfit and DoGray; version 1 additionally supports SymmetricGridfit and SymmetricSmoothing. Os2Table.EmbeddingFlags Embedding permission flags stored in FsType. Os2Table.SelectionFlags Font style selection flags stored in FsSelection.

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.TtfEncoderFactory.ht.title: Class TtfEncoderFactory | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.TtfEncoderFactory.ht.summary: Class TtfEncoderFactory Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF Assembly Utils.Fonts.dll A factory for selecting the appropriate text encoding for TrueType name table entries. The encoding is determined by the triplet (PlatformId, PlatformSpecificId, LanguageId). public static class TtfEncoderFactory Inheritance object TtfEncoderFactory Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods GetEncoding(TtfPlatFormId, TtfPlatformSpecificID, TtfLanguageID) Returns the appropriate Encoding based on the specified platform ID and platform-specific ID. public static Encoding GetEncoding(TtfPlatFormId platformID, TtfPlatformSpecificID platformSpecificID, TtfLanguageID languageID) Parameters platformID TtfPlatFormId The TrueType platform ID. platformSpecificID TtfPlatformSpecificID The TrueType platform-specific ID. languageID TtfLanguageID The TrueType language ID. Per the OpenType 'name' table spec, the byte encoding of a record never depends on its language: this parameter only identifies which language the string is written in (e.g. English vs. French), not how its bytes are laid out. It is accepted for API symmetry with the documented (PlatformID, PlatformSpecificID, LanguageID) triplet but intentionally unused here. Returns Encoding The selected Encoding. For Macintosh with the Roman script, this returns MacRoman (code page 10000) if available, otherwise ASCII. For Microsoft, most encoding IDs use Big-Endian Unicode (UTF-16BE), except the legacy CJK double-byte encodings (ShiftJIS, PRC, Big5, Wansung, Johab), which use their respective single/double-byte code pages. The Unicode platform always uses UTF-16BE.

api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.TTF.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts.TTF Classes HeadTable The 'head' table contains global information about the font. It records facts such as the font version number, creation and modification dates, revision number, and basic typographic data that applies to the font as a whole. This includes the font bounding box, the primary writing direction, and other information regarding glyph placement within the em square. The checksum is used to verify the integrity of the font data and can help distinguish between similar fonts. LocaRecord Represents a record from the 'loca' table of a TrueType font. NameTable The name table (tag: 'name') allows inclusion of human‑readable names for features and settings, copyright notices, font names, style names, and other information related to the font. These name strings can be provided in any language, and are referenced by other TrueType tables. NameTable.NameRecord Represents a record in the name table, defined by the triplet (PlatformId, PlatformSpecificId, LanguageId) and a NameId. TTFPoint Represents a point in the TrueType glyph space. TTFTableAttribute Indicates the TrueType table associated with a class and specifies its dependencies. TableTypes Provides constants for known OpenType table tags. Includes well-known system tables such as "cmap", "glyf", "name", etc. TrueTypeFont Represents a TrueType font. TrueTypeFont.TableDeclaration Represents a declaration of a table in the TrueType font. TrueTypeGlyph Represents a TrueType glyph and implements the IGlyph interface. This class acts as a wrapper around a GlyphBase instance, delegating rendering and metric calculations. TrueTypeTable Represents the base class for a TrueType table. TtfEncoderFactory A factory for selecting the appropriate text encoding for TrueType name table entries. The encoding is determined by the triplet (PlatformId, PlatformSpecificId, LanguageId). Structs Tag Represents a 4-byte identifier used in font file formats such as TrueType (TTF). Encodes a string of up to 4 ASCII characters into a 32-bit integer. Provides implicit conversions and equality comparisons with strings, integers, and byte arrays. Enums TableTypes.Tags Tags for OpenType font tables, using their 4-byte integer encodings.

All paths are then exposed through the IDrawable interface so that a single fill call covers the whole string. UV coordinates produced by the fill are normalised over the bounding box of the entire text block, making texture or gradient effects straightforward: draw.FillShape2((u, v) => texture[(int)(u * w), (int)(v * h)], text); Constructors Text(string, IFont, float) Initializes a new Text instance using the font's own scale and baseline metrics. public Text(string content, IFont font, float x) Parameters content string The string to render. font IFont Font providing both glyph outlines and metrics (Scale, BaseLineY). x float Horizontal pen position of the first character in pixels. Typically computed as topY + ascent * scale using the font's ascender value. scale float Factor mapping font units to pixels, usually desiredPixelHeight / unitsPerEm. The Y axis is automatically flipped to convert from TrueType space (Y-up) to screen space (Y-down). Always guard a comparison against one of these two members with a check on TtfPlatFormId first (see GetEncoding(TtfPlatFormId, TtfPlatformSpecificID, TtfLanguageID) for the correct pattern) -- comparing a platform-specific ID to UNICODE_DEFAULT without checking the platform first would also match a Macintosh Roman-script record.

api/Utils.Fonts.html.title: Namespace Utils.Fonts | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Fonts.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Fonts Classes FontSupport Provides utilities and encoding tables for working with font character sets and glyph name mappings. Includes standard encodings (WinAnsi, MacRoman, ISO Latin1, etc.) and predefined glyph names used in Type 1 and Type 1C fonts. Paths<T> Collects drawing paths generated while converting glyph outlines. Text Lays out a string of text as a set of filled glyph outlines and implements IDrawable so that it can be passed directly to fill methods such as FillShape2(UVMap<T>, params IDrawable[]). Interfaces IFont Represents a generic font abstraction capable of retrieving glyphs and applying spacing corrections. IGlyph Represents a glyph in a font, with dimensions and rendering capabilities. IGraphicConverter Defines a graphics drawing interface for rendering glyph vector outlines. Enums CompoundGlyfFlags Flags for compound glyph components. FontBaseLine Indicates baseline positioning for typesetting in different writing systems. FontDirectionHintEnum Indicates glyph layout directionality hints. FontFlags Flags indicating various font characteristics. FontStyle Indicates stylistic attributes of a font. Le Flags permet de combiner Bold et Italic pour obtenir, par exemple, du BoldItalic. FontType Identifies the underlying technology or format of a font resource. HeadFlags Flags used in the 'head' table of a TrueType font. MacStyleFlags macStyle field flags from the 'head' table. OutlineFlags Outline point flags used in glyph definitions. TtfLanguageID Lists the language identifiers used by TrueType name records for localization. TtfNameID Represents the name identifiers stored in a TrueType font name table. TtfPlatFormId Enumerates the platform families that can appear in TrueType name records. TtfPlatformSpecificID Identifies the platform-specific encodings associated with a TtfPlatFormId.

api/Utils.Format.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Format Classes CustomFormatter Provides a customisable ICustomFormatter implementation backed by delegates registered per type. FormatProviderEx Extension helpers that expose strongly typed IFormatProvider data such as TextInfo or NumberFormatInfo without repeatedly casting the results of GetFormat(Type). NullFormatter Provides a minimal ICustomFormatter that forwards formatting to the wrapped provider or ToString(). StringFormatBuilder Builds string-format delegates while compiling embedded expressions with an injected compiler. Interfaces IStringFormatBuilder Defines an injectable builder for creating compiled string-format delegates.

MapPoint(ProjectedPoint<T>, byte, int) Initializes a new instance of the MapPoint<T> class from projected coordinates. public MapPoint(ProjectedPoint<T> projectedPoint, byte zoomLevel, int tileSize) Parameters projectedPoint ProjectedPoint<T> Projected map coordinates. zoomLevel byte Target zoom level. tileSize int Tile size in pixels. Remarks Uses Normalize(ProjectedPoint<T>) (via Projection) to convert the projected point into [0,1] map-fraction coordinates before scaling by the total map size in pixels (tileSize << zoomLevel, computed in long to avoid overflowing int at high zoom levels), matching GetMapSize(byte) and MappointToTile(ProjectedPoint<T>, byte) exactly, so both paths agree on which tile a given point falls into. The resulting pixel coordinates are clamped to [0, mapSize - 1]: a normalized value of exactly 1 (e.g. Equirectangular's latitude 90°, or Mercator's own MaxLatitude) would otherwise floor to mapSize, one pixel past the last valid one. Properties Tile Gets the tile that contains this point. public Tile<T> Tile { get; } Property Value Tile<T> Remarks Uses floored division (via TileX/TileY) rather than truncated integer division, so that negative coordinates resolve to the correct tile instead of rounding towards zero. TileSize Gets or sets the tile size, in pixels, used for the grid. public int TileSize { get; set; } Property Value int TileX Gets the X coordinate within the tile, always in the range [0, TileSize). public int TileX { get; } Property Value int TileY Gets the Y coordinate within the tile, always in the range [0, TileSize). public int TileY { get; } Property Value int X Gets or sets the projected X coordinate in pixels. public long X { get; set; } Property Value long Y Gets or sets the projected Y coordinate in pixels. public long Y { get; set; } Property Value long ZoomLevel Gets or sets the zoom level associated with the point. public byte ZoomLevel { get; set; } Property Value byte

Returns T Ground resolution (meters per pixel) at the specified latitude. GeoPointToMappoint(GeoPoint<T>, byte) Converts a geographic point to its projected representation. public ProjectedPoint<T> GeoPointToMappoint(GeoPoint<T> geoPoint, byte zoomFactor) Parameters geoPoint GeoPoint<T> Geographic coordinates. zoomFactor byte Zoom factor (ignored, maintained for backward compatibility). Returns ProjectedPoint<T> The projected map point. GetMapSize(byte) Gets the total map size (in pixels) for the specified zoom level. public long GetMapSize(byte zoomLevel) Parameters zoomLevel byte Zoom level of the tile grid. Returns long Total map size in pixels. Remarks Returns long (rather than int) because TileSize << zoomLevel overflows a 32-bit int at realistic zoom levels — e.g. with the default 256px tile size, zoom 24 already overflows. MappointToGeoPoint(ProjectedPoint<T>) Converts a projected map point back to geographic coordinates. public GeoPoint<T> MappointToGeoPoint(ProjectedPoint<T> projectedPoint) Parameters projectedPoint ProjectedPoint<T> Projected map point. Returns GeoPoint<T> Geographic coordinates. MappointToTile(ProjectedPoint<T>, byte) Converts a projected map point to tile coordinates at the specified zoom level. public Tile<T> MappointToTile(ProjectedPoint<T> projectedPoint, byte zoomLevel) Parameters projectedPoint ProjectedPoint<T> Projected map point. zoomLevel byte Zoom level of the tile grid. Returns Tile<T> The tile containing the projected point. Remarks Uses Normalize(ProjectedPoint<T>) to convert the projected point into [0,1] map-fraction coordinates before scaling by GetMapSize(byte), matching MapPoint<T>'s own pixel calculation exactly, so both agree on which tile a given point falls into. The resulting pixel coordinates are clamped to [0, mapSize - 1] before dividing by TileSize: a normalized value of exactly 1 (e.g. Equirectangular's latitude 90°, or Mercator's own MaxLatitude) would otherwise floor to mapSize, one tile past the last valid one — the same edge case MapPoint<T> guards against.
