api/Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Affi.summary: Class AffineSubspace<T> Namespace Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra Assembly Utils.Mathematics.dll Represents a k-dimensional affine subspace (flat) embedded in a d-dimensional ambient space. public sealed class AffineSubspace<T> : IEquatable<AffineSubspace<T>>, ICloneable, IFormattable where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IPowerFunctions<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Type Parameters T Floating-point scalar type. Inheritance object AffineSubspace<T> Implements IEquatable<AffineSubspace<T>> ICloneable IFormattable Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) The internal representation is an orthonormal basis computed by Gram–Schmidt. Numerical stability is limited, not unconditional (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass4.md item #50): rank/independence decisions during construction, and the tolerance-based comparisons in IntersectWith(AffineSubspace<T>)/IntersectWith(Line<T>)/Equals(AffineSubspace<T>?), use a fixed absolute epsilon (1e-10) rather than a scale-aware relative tolerance, and there is no re-orthogonalization pass or conditioning/residual diagnostic. This is adequate for moderate-magnitude coordinates and clearly independent inputs, but nearly dependent directions/normals or large coordinate scales can silently produce an incorrect rank or a looser-than-expected membership test. FromSpan(Vector<T>, T?, params Vector<T>[])/FromNormals(Vector<T>, T?, params Vector<T>[]) accept an optional rankTolerance override for callers who need to tune this threshold for their own scale; a fully scale-aware/SVD-based construction was not implemented (see the item's "Fixed" note for why). Special cases: dimension 0 is a single point; dimension d is the full ambient space. Properties AmbientDimension Gets the dimension of the ambient space. public int AmbientDimension { get; } Property Value int Anchor Gets the anchor point that lies on the subspace. public Vector<T> Anchor { get; } Property Value Vector<T> Codimension Gets the codimension (= AmbientDimension − Dimension). public int Codimension { get; } Property Value int Dimension Gets the dimension of this subspace. public int Dimension { get; } Property Value int Methods Clone() Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance. public object Clone() Returns object A new object that is a copy of this instance. Contains(Vector<T>, T) Determines whether point lies on this subspace within the given tolerance. public bool Contains(Vector<T> point, T tolerance) Parameters point Vector<T> Point to test. tolerance T Maximum allowable perpendicular distance. Must be finite and non-negative. Returns bool true if the point is within tolerance of the subspace. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when point is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when tolerance is not finite or is negative: an unchecked negative tolerance would reject even an exact member, NaN would make every comparison false, and positive infinity would silently accept every finite-distance point as a member (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass4.md item #48). DistanceTo(Vector<T>) Computes the shortest distance from point to this subspace. public T DistanceTo(Vector<T> point) Parameters point Vector<T> Point to measure from. Must have the same ambient dimension. Returns T The perpendicular distance. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when point is null. ArgumentException Thrown when the point has a different ambient dimension. Equals(object?) Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current object. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object The object to compare with the current object. Returns bool true if the specified object is equal to the current object; otherwise, false. Equals(AffineSubspace<T>?) Determines whether other represents the same affine subspace as this one. Two subspaces are equal when they have the same dimension and the same point set (i.e., other's anchor and all its direction vectors lie in this subspace). public bool Equals(AffineSubspace<T>? other) Parameters other AffineSubspace<T> Subspace to compare. Returns bool true if both subspaces are geometrically identical. FromNormals(Vector<T>, T?, params Vector<T>[]) Creates an affine subspace of dimension d − k defined by k normal vectors. The subspace is the set of points whose displacement from anchor is orthogonal to every supplied normal. Linearly dependent normals are silently discarded. public static AffineSubspace<T> FromNormals(Vector<T> anchor, T? rankTolerance, params Vector<T>[] normals) Parameters anchor Vector<T> A point on the subspace. rankTolerance T? Overrides the default fixed absolute epsilon (1e-10) used to decide whether a normal is linearly independent of those already accepted, and to decide when a candidate complement direction has collapsed to zero (see this type's remarks on numerical stability, TODO-2026-07-11-pass4.md item #50). Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. normals Vector<T>[] Normal vectors. Each independent normal reduces the dimension by one. Returns AffineSubspace<T> The affine subspace through anchor orthogonal to the given normals. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when anchor, normals, or any element of normals is null. ArgumentException Thrown when no normals are supplied or a normal has the wrong ambient dimension. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when rankTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. FromNormals(Vector<T>, params Vector<T>[]) Creates an affine subspace of dimension d − k defined by k normal vectors, using the default rank tolerance. See FromNormals(Vector<T>, T?, params Vector<T>[]) for an overload accepting an explicit tolerance override. public static AffineSubspace<T> FromNormals(Vector<T> anchor, params Vector<T>[] normals) Parameters anchor Vector<T> A point on the subspace. normals Vector<T>[] Normal vectors. Each independent normal reduces the dimension by one. Returns AffineSubspace<T> The affine subspace through anchor orthogonal to the given normals. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when anchor, normals, or any element of normals is null. ArgumentException Thrown when no normals are supplied or a normal has the wrong ambient dimension. FromSpan(Vector<T>, T?, params Vector<T>[]) Creates an affine subspace of dimension k spanned by k direction vectors. Linearly dependent inputs are silently discarded; the resulting dimension equals the rank. public static AffineSubspace<T> FromSpan(Vector<T> anchor, T? rankTolerance, params Vector<T>[] directions) Parameters anchor Vector<T> A point on the subspace. rankTolerance T? Overrides the default fixed absolute epsilon (1e-10) used to decide whether a direction is linearly independent of those already accepted (see this type's remarks on numerical stability, TODO-2026-07-11-pass4.md item #50). Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. directions Vector<T>[] Spanning directions. At least one must be non-zero. Returns AffineSubspace<T> The affine subspace through anchor in the given directions. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when anchor, directions, or any element of directions is null. ArgumentException Thrown when no directions are supplied, a direction has the wrong ambient dimension, or all directions are zero / mutually collinear. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when rankTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. FromSpan(Vector<T>, params Vector<T>[]) Creates an affine subspace of dimension k spanned by k direction vectors, using the default rank tolerance. See FromSpan(Vector<T>, T?, params Vector<T>[]) for an overload accepting an explicit tolerance override. public static AffineSubspace<T> FromSpan(Vector<T> anchor, params Vector<T>[] directions) Parameters anchor Vector<T> A point on the subspace. directions Vector<T>[] Spanning directions. At least one must be non-zero. Returns AffineSubspace<T> The affine subspace through anchor in the given directions. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when anchor, directions, or any element of directions is null. ArgumentException Thrown when no directions are supplied, a direction has the wrong ambient dimension, or all directions are zero / mutually collinear. GetHashCode() Combines only Dimension and AmbientDimension. This is a deliberately coarse hash, not an oversight (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass4.md item #51): Equals(AffineSubspace<T>?) is a tolerance-based geometric comparison (same point set, not same anchor/basis representation), so a hash that is actually consistent with it would need to derive a value invariant across every representation of the same subspace - and stable across the boundary where two subspaces stop being "close enough" to compare equal - which the fixed-epsilon geometric equality this type uses cannot support without also risking hash/equals inconsistency (two subspaces that compare equal hashing differently because their real-valued invariants fall in different quantization buckets). This satisfies the required equal-implies-same-hash contract, but every subspace of the same dimension/ambient dimension collides: this makes hash-based collections (HashSet<T>, dictionary keys) degrade to near-linear lookup for a collection containing many subspaces. Callers needing better distribution should key such collections by an application-specific canonical representation (e.g. a snapped/rounded anchor plus basis) via a dedicated IEqualityComparer<T> instead of relying on this type's own hash. public override int GetHashCode() Returns int IntersectWith(AffineSubspace<T>) Computes the intersection of this subspace with another affine subspace. public AffineSubspace<T>? IntersectWith(AffineSubspace<T> other) Parameters other AffineSubspace<T> The other affine subspace. Must share the same ambient dimension. Property Value T The element at the specified position. Rows Gets the number of rows in the matrix. public int Rows { get; } Property Value int Methods Clone() Creates a copy of the matrix. public object Clone() Returns object ComputeEigenvalues(int, T?, T?, T?) Computes the real eigenvalues and corresponding eigenvectors of a symmetric matrix using the QR iteration algorithm. public (T[] Eigenvalues, Matrix<T> Eigenvectors) ComputeEigenvalues(int maxIterations = 1000, T? convergenceTolerance = null, T? symmetryTolerance = null, T? rankTolerance = null) Parameters maxIterations int Maximum number of QR iterations before giving up. Must be greater than zero. convergenceTolerance T? Overrides the default relative-plus-absolute convergence tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)) used to decide when the off-diagonal magnitude is small enough to stop iterating. When supplied, the effective absolute threshold is this value multiplied by the matrix's largest entry. Independently configurable from symmetryTolerance and rankTolerance. Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. symmetryTolerance T? Overrides the default tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)) used by the upfront IsSymmetric(T?) input-validation check. Forwarded directly to IsSymmetric(T?); independently configurable from convergenceTolerance and rankTolerance. Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. rankTolerance T? Overrides the default tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)) used by each QR iteration's internal DecomposeQR(T?) call to decide whether a column is already numerically rank-deficient. Forwarded directly to DecomposeQR(T?) at every iteration; independently configurable from convergenceTolerance and symmetryTolerance. Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. Returns (T[] Eigenvalues, Matrix<T> Eigenvectors) A tuple containing an array of eigenvalues (descending by magnitude) and a matrix whose columns are the corresponding eigenvectors. Remarks Only real symmetric matrices are supported; all eigenvalues of such matrices are guaranteed real. Eigenvalues are returned in descending order of absolute value. Uses the symmetric QR algorithm with a Wilkinson shift and deflation: each step shifts the active (not-yet-deflated) leading submatrix by an estimate of the eigenvalue closest to its trailing corner before applying A ← R·Q, which - unlike plain unshifted iteration - gives locally cubic convergence and remains fast even for closely-spaced (clustered) eigenvalues. Once the last row/column of the active submatrix decouples (goes to zero within tolerance), that eigenvalue is locked in and the active submatrix shrinks by one, so later eigenvalues do not have to pay for the cost of resolving earlier ones. Known limitation: unlike a textbook implementation, this does not first reduce the matrix to tridiagonal form, so each QR step still costs O(m³) for the current active size m (via DecomposeQR(T?)) rather than the O(m) a tridiagonal reduction would allow. This affects performance, not correctness or convergence rate: the shift is still computed from (and applied to) the true active submatrix at each step. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when maxIterations is not positive, or any of convergenceTolerance, symmetryTolerance, rankTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. InvalidOperationException Thrown when the matrix is not square, not symmetric, or fails to converge. DecomposeQR(T?) Performs a QR decomposition of this matrix using Householder reflections. public (Matrix<T> Q, Matrix<T> R) DecomposeQR(T? rankTolerance = null) Parameters rankTolerance T? Overrides the default relative-plus-absolute tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)) used to decide whether a column's remaining sub-diagonal component is already numerically zero (rank-deficient at that step). When supplied, the effective absolute threshold is this value multiplied by the matrix's largest entry. Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. Returns (Matrix<T> Q, Matrix<T> R) A tuple containing the orthogonal matrix Q and the upper-triangular matrix R. Remarks For an m×n matrix A with m ≥ n, returns Q (m×n, orthonormal columns) and R (n×n, upper triangular) such that A = Q·R. Unlike a Gram-Schmidt-based decomposition, Householder reflections remain well-defined when a column is linearly dependent on the ones before it: a rank-deficient column simply produces a (numerically) zero diagonal entry in R at that step rather than requiring the decomposition to be rejected, which is what allows singular square matrices to reach ComputeEigenvalues(int, T?, T?, T?). Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when the matrix has more columns than rows. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when rankTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. Diagonal(params IEnumerable<T>) Returns a square diagonal matrix whose diagonal entries are values. public static Matrix<T> Diagonal(params IEnumerable<T> values) Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Diagonal values, left to right. Returns Matrix<T> DiagonalizeLU(T?) Performs a pivoted LU decomposition of the current square matrix: a lower unitriangular matrix L, an upper triangular matrix U, and a permutation matrix P such that P * A = L * U, where A is the current matrix. public (Matrix<T> L, Matrix<T> U, Matrix<T> P) DiagonalizeLU(T? relativeSingularityTolerance = null) Parameters relativeSingularityTolerance T? Overrides the default relative-plus-absolute pivot tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)) used to reject a numerically near-singular matrix; see Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.ResolveDecompositionTolerance(T[,], T?). Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. Returns (Matrix<T> L, Matrix<T> U, Matrix<T> P) A tuple containing the lower-triangular, upper-triangular, and permutation matrices. Remarks Delegates to the pivoted elimination shared with Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.ComputeDeterminant(), Solve(Vector<T>, T?), and Invert(T?) (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass5.md item #69). Unlike Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.ComputeDeterminant(), this uses the scale-aware relative-plus-absolute-floor tolerance (see Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.TryDecomposePivoted(T, out Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.PivotedElimination)'s remarks), since L/U are meant to be used for further numerically reliable computation, not just an exact product of pivots. Structural metadata on the returned matrices reflects only what is mathematically guaranteed for every input: L's unit diagonal makes it always triangular with determinant one, and U is always triangular; whether L, U, or P also happen to be diagonal or the identity (e.g. when no pivoting was needed, or the source was already triangular) is left to lazy recomputation rather than hardcoded as false (see item #61/#68). P's determinant (the sign of the permutation) is known directly from the elimination's swap count, so it is supplied rather than left for recomputation. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when the matrix is not square or is singular. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when relativeSingularityTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. Equals(object?) A correct hash implementation guarantees equal values hash equally, so a hash precondition here adds no correctness value; it only makes it hazardous to later introduce a tolerance-aware equality or hashing policy where two "equal" matrices might legitimately hash differently. When supplied, the effective absolute threshold is this value multiplied by the matrix's largest entry (no absolute floor is added, unlike the default). Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. Remarks Delegates to the pivoted elimination shared with DiagonalizeLU(T?), Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.ComputeDeterminant(), and Solve(Vector<T>, T?) (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass5.md item #69), using the same scale-aware tolerance as DiagonalizeLU(T?)/Solve(Vector<T>, T?) (not Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.ComputeDeterminant()'s exact-zero check - see Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.TryDecomposePivoted(T, out Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matrix<T>.PivotedElimination)'s remarks): column j of the inverse is the solution of A·x = e_j for the j-th standard basis vector, computed via the same forward/back substitution as Solve(Vector<T>, T?). Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when the matrix is not square. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when relativeSingularityTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. IsDiagonalWithin(T) Indicates whether the matrix is diagonal within tolerance. See IsTriangularWithin(T) for the tolerance-vs-exact rationale. public bool IsDiagonalWithin(T tolerance) Parameters tolerance T Maximum absolute value an off-diagonal entry may have and still be treated as zero. Must be finite and non-negative. Returns bool Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when tolerance is not finite or is negative. IsIdentityWithin(T) Indicates whether the matrix is the identity matrix within tolerance, treating diagonal entries within tolerance of one, and off-diagonal entries within tolerance of zero, as matching. See IsTriangularWithin(T) for the tolerance-vs-exact rationale. public bool IsIdentityWithin(T tolerance) Parameters tolerance T Maximum allowed absolute deviation from the expected exact value. Must be finite and non-negative. Returns bool Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when tolerance is not finite or is negative. IsNormalSpaceWithin(T) Indicates whether the matrix represents a normal space within tolerance. See IsTriangularWithin(T) for the tolerance-vs-exact rationale. public bool IsNormalSpaceWithin(T tolerance) Parameters tolerance T Maximum allowed absolute deviation from the expected exact value. Must be finite and non-negative. Returns bool Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when tolerance is not finite or is negative. IsSymmetric() Returns true when this square matrix is symmetric (A[i,j] == A[j,i]) within the default relative-plus-absolute tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)). public bool IsSymmetric() Returns bool IsSymmetric(T?) Returns true when this square matrix is symmetric (A[i,j] == A[j,i]) within symmetryTolerance. public bool IsSymmetric(T? symmetryTolerance) Parameters symmetryTolerance T? Overrides the default relative-plus-absolute tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)) used to compare A[i,j] to A[j,i]. When supplied, the effective absolute threshold is this value multiplied by the matrix's largest entry. This is independent of ComputeEigenvalues(int, T?, T?, T?)'s (separately configurable) convergence tolerance. Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. Returns bool Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when symmetryTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. IsTriangularWithin(T) Indicates whether the matrix is triangular (upper or lower) within tolerance, treating any off-diagonal entry with absolute value at most tolerance as zero. Unlike IsTriangular, which requires exact zero, this tolerates rounding noise left over from prior arithmetic or decomposition; it is a separate, explicitly opt-in predicate rather than a silently chosen global tolerance applied to IsTriangular. public bool IsTriangularWithin(T tolerance) Parameters tolerance T Maximum absolute value an off-diagonal entry may have and still be treated as zero. Must be finite and non-negative. Returns bool Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when tolerance is not finite or is negative. Pad(int, int) Resizes the matrix to the specified new dimensions, copying the overlapping top-left prefix and zero-filling any newly added rows/columns. public Matrix<T> Pad(int newRows, int newColumns) Parameters newRows int The new number of rows. Must be positive. newColumns int The new number of columns. Must be positive. Returns Matrix<T> A new matrix with the requested dimensions. Remarks Despite its name, this is a resize, not a pure enlargement: whenever newRows and/or newColumns is smaller than the corresponding current dimension, the returned matrix crops that dimension down (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass5.md item #67) instead of throwing or requiring the new dimensions to be at least the current ones. Only the overlapping min(current, new) prefix of rows/columns is preserved either way. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when newRows or newColumns is not positive. Solve(Vector<T>, T?) Solves the linear system A·x = b and returns x. Uses Gaussian elimination with partial pivoting. public Vector<T> Solve(Vector<T> b, T? relativeSingularityTolerance = null) Parameters b Vector<T> Right-hand-side vector. Its dimension must equal the number of rows. relativeSingularityTolerance T? Overrides the default relative-plus-absolute pivot tolerance (see DefaultTolerance(T, int)) used to reject a numerically near-singular matrix. When supplied, the effective absolute threshold is this value multiplied by the matrix's largest entry (no absolute floor is added, unlike the default); pass a smaller value to accept more ill-conditioned systems, or a larger value to reject them more aggressively. Must be finite and non-negative when supplied. Returns Vector<T> The solution vector x. Remarks Delegates to the pivoted elimination shared with DiagonalizeLU(T?), Determinant, and Invert(T?) (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass5.md item #69), using the same scale-aware tolerance as DiagonalizeLU(T?)/Invert(T?) (not Determinant's exact-zero check - see TryDecomposePivoted's remarks): A·x = b becomes L·U·x = P·b (since P·A = L·U), solved by forward substitution for y in L·y = P·b followed by back substitution for x in U·x = y. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when the matrix is not square or is singular. ArgumentException Thrown when b has the wrong dimension. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when relativeSingularityTolerance is supplied but not finite or is negative. ToArray() Converts the matrix to a 2D array. public T[,] ToArray() Returns T[,] ToString() Returns a string representation of the matrix. public override string ToString() Returns string ToString(string?) Returns a formatted string representation of the matrix. public string ToString(string? format) Parameters format string Returns string ToString(string?, IFormatProvider?) Returns a formatted string representation of the matrix using the specified format provider. public string ToString(string? format, IFormatProvider? formatProvider) Parameters format string A composite format string: an optional layout token ("", "S", "C", or "SC", selecting the row separator) optionally followed by : and a standard numeric format string (e.g. "S:F2") forwarded verbatim to each element's own ToString(string, IFormatProvider). When no numeric format is given, elements are formatted with their own default (null) format rather than being rounded to a culture-dependent number of decimals first: unlike the previous behavior, this never silently discards precision the caller did not ask to lose. formatProvider IFormatProvider Culture or number-format info controlling the row/column separators and forwarded to each element's own formatter alongside the numeric format. api/Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra.Matr.summary: Class MatrixTransformations Namespace Utils.Mathematics.LinearAlgebra Assembly Utils.Mathematics.dll Provides factory methods to create common transformation matrices. public static class MatrixTransformations Inheritance object MatrixTransformations Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Diagonal<T>(params IEnumerable<T>) Creates a diagonal matrix from the provided values. public static Matrix<T> Diagonal<T>(params IEnumerable<T> values) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Diagonal values. Returns Matrix<T> New diagonal matrix. Type Parameters T Numeric type of the matrix. Remarks Delegates to Diagonal(params IEnumerable<T>), which is the single implementation of diagonal-matrix construction: an earlier, independent implementation here cached isTriangular/isDiagonal as false whenever any diagonal value was zero, even though a matrix with zero diagonal entries is still diagonal and triangular by definition (only its invertibility/determinant changes). Two independently maintained factories for the same construction had already drifted into different, incorrect behavior; keeping one implementation avoids a repeat. Identity<T>(int) Creates an identity matrix of the specified dimension. public static Matrix<T> Identity<T>(int dimension) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters dimension int Matrix dimension. Must be positive. Returns Matrix<T> New identity matrix. Type Parameters T Numeric type of the matrix. Remarks Delegates to Identity(int), which is the single implementation of identity-matrix construction: an earlier, independent implementation here performed no validation at all, so a zero dimension silently built a 0×0 matrix and a negative dimension failed through raw array allocation instead of a clean, documented exception — disagreeing with Identity(int)'s domain and exception behavior for the same operation (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass5.md item #62). Two independently maintained factories for the same construction had already drifted once for Diagonal<T>(params IEnumerable<T>); keeping one implementation avoids a repeat. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when dimension is not positive. Rotation<T>(params IEnumerable<T>) Generates a rotation matrix. public static Matrix<T> Rotation<T>(params IEnumerable<T> angles) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, ITrigonometricFunctions<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters angles IEnumerable<T> Angles of rotation, one per axis pair of the base d × d rotation block: d * (d - 1) / 2 angles are required for a given base dimension d. Must not be empty: with zero angles, the d * (d - 1) / 2 = n formula solves to the degenerate d = 1 (a meaningless "1×1 rotation", since rotation requires at least two axes to rotate between) rather than identifying which ambient dimension's identity rotation the caller intended (see TODO-2026-07-11-pass5.md item #66). To build an identity matrix of a specific dimension, call Identity<T>(int) directly instead. Returns Matrix<T> New rotation matrix. Type Parameters T Numeric type of the matrix. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when angles is empty, or its count does not match d * (d - 1) / 2 for any integer d. Scaling<T>(params IEnumerable<T>) Generates a scaling matrix. public static Matrix<T> Scaling<T>(params IEnumerable<T> coefficients) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters coefficients IEnumerable<T> Scaling factors for each axis. Returns Matrix<T> New scaling matrix. Type Parameters T Numeric type of the matrix. Skew<T>(params IEnumerable<T>) Generates a shear (skew) matrix using the provided angles. public static Matrix<T> Skew<T>(params IEnumerable<T> angles) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, ITrigonometricFunctions<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters angles IEnumerable<T> One angle per off-diagonal coefficient of the base d × d shear block, in row-major order skipping the diagonal (row 0's d-1 off-diagonal columns, then row 1's, and so on). The base dimension d is inferred from the count: d * (d - 1) angles are required, since a full shear has one free coefficient per off-diagonal position. Returns Matrix<T> A new (d+1) × (d+1) homogeneous shear matrix: identity everywhere except the supplied tan(angle) values at the base block's off-diagonal positions. Type Parameters T Numeric type of the matrix. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when the angle count does not match d * (d - 1) for any integer d. Transform<T>(params IEnumerable<T>) Generates a general affine transformation matrix. public static Matrix<T> Transform<T>(params IEnumerable<T> values) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Coefficients for the upper d × (d+1) affine block, in row-major order: for each of the d base-dimension rows, d linear coefficients followed by that row's translation coefficient (last column). The base dimension d is inferred from the count: d * (d + 1) values are required. For example, a 2D transform needs 6 values [a, b, tx, c, d, ty], producing the matrix rows [a, b, tx], [c, d, ty]. Returns Matrix<T> A new (d+1) × (d+1) homogeneous transformation matrix: the supplied coefficients fill the upper affine block, and the final row is left as the homogeneous row [0, …, 0, 1], consistent with this library's matrix-times-column-vector multiplication convention (the same convention Translation<T>(params IEnumerable<T>) uses for its translation column). Type Parameters T Numeric type of the matrix. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when the value count does not match d * (d + 1) for any integer d. Translation<T>(params IEnumerable<T>) Generates a translation matrix. public static Matrix<T> Translation<T>(params IEnumerable<T> values) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Translation values. Returns Matrix<T> New translation matrix. Type Parameters T Numeric type of the matrix.

Properties Dimension Gets the vector dimension. public int Dimension { get; } Property Value int this[int] Gets the value of the specified component. public T this[int dimension] { get; } Parameters dimension int Component index. Property Value T Norm Gets the length of the vector. public T Norm { get; } Property Value T Remarks Computed via a scaled sum-of-squares accumulation (the same running-scale technique as BLAS nrm2/hypot), rather than summing component * component directly. A direct sum can overflow to infinity for large-but-representable components (whose square individually overflows even though the true norm does not), or underflow to zero for small-but-representable components (whose square underflows to zero even though it contributes to a representable norm). Tracking the largest component seen so far as a running scale and accumulating only the ratio of each component to that scale keeps every intermediate value within a representable range. api/Utils.Mathematics.NumericalMethods.h.summary: Class NumericalMethods Namespace Utils.Mathematics Assembly Utils.Mathematics.dll Numerical integration and interpolation utilities. public static class NumericalMethods Inheritance object NumericalMethods Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Integrate<T>(Func<T, T>, T, T, int) Numerically integrates f over [a, b] using the composite Simpson's 1/3 rule. public static T Integrate<T>(Func<T, T> f, T a, T b, int steps = 1000) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T> Parameters f Func<T, T> Integrand function. Must be defined and finite on [a, b]. a T Lower bound of integration. b T Upper bound of integration. steps int Number of sub-intervals. Must be positive and even; an odd value is rounded up by one. Higher values increase accuracy at the cost of more function evaluations. Returns T The approximate value of ∫f(x)dx from a to b. If f returns a non-finite value anywhere in the interval, that non-finite value propagates into the result (visible to the caller as NaN or an infinity) rather than being silently discarded. Type Parameters T Floating-point scalar type. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when f is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when steps is not positive, when steps is the odd value MaxValue (which cannot be rounded up to an even count without overflowing), or when a/b is not finite. Lagrange<T>(IEnumerable<(T x, T y)>, T) Evaluates the Lagrange interpolating polynomial at at, given a set of data points. public static T Lagrange<T>(IEnumerable<(T x, T y)> points, T at) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T> Parameters points IEnumerable<(T x, T y)> Sequence of (x, y) data points. All x values must be distinct and every coordinate finite. at T The point at which to evaluate the interpolating polynomial. Must be finite. Returns T The interpolated value at at. Type Parameters T Floating-point scalar type. Remarks The result is exact at each data point and provides a polynomial approximation elsewhere. For large point sets, consider alternative methods as Lagrange interpolation can exhibit Runge's phenomenon near the boundaries. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when no points are provided, a coordinate is not finite, or x values are not distinct. ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when at is not finite.

api/Utils.Mathematics.Statistics.html.summary: Class Statistics Namespace Utils.Mathematics Assembly Utils.Mathematics.dll Descriptive statistics computed over sequences of floating-point values. public static class Statistics Inheritance object Statistics Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Non-finite value policy: every operation here that constrains T to IFloatingPoint<TSelf> validates each input value with T.IsFinite(value) as it is enumerated and rejects the sequence with an ArgumentException on the first NaN or infinite value found, rather than letting it propagate into a NaN/infinite (and implementation-dependent, since propagation depends on where the value falls relative to sort order or accumulation order) result. This is a single consistent policy applied uniformly across Mean<T>(IEnumerable<T>), Variance<T>(IEnumerable<T>), Covariance<T>(IEnumerable<T>, IEnumerable<T>), and Correlation<T>(IEnumerable<T>, IEnumerable<T>). Median<T>(IEnumerable<T>) is generic over any IComparable<T>, not just floating-point types, so it cannot apply the same finiteness check; its behavior for a type whose CompareTo(T) does not define a total order (floating-point NaN under the default comparer being the standard example) is therefore whatever Sort<T>(T[]) does with such values, which is unspecified. Validate finiteness before calling if that matters. Methods Correlation<T>(IEnumerable<T>, IEnumerable<T>) Returns the Pearson correlation coefficient between two sequences of equal length. public static T Correlation<T>(IEnumerable<T> x, IEnumerable<T> y) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters x IEnumerable<T> First sequence, all values finite. y IEnumerable<T> Second sequence, all values finite. Returns T The correlation coefficient in [−1, 1]. Type Parameters T Floating-point element type. Remarks Computes both variances and the covariance in a single online pass (rather than materializing both sequences and separately calling Covariance<T>(IEnumerable<T>, IEnumerable<T>) and StdDev<T>(IEnumerable<T>), each of which would re-enumerate and independently recompute its own mean). Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when the sequences have different lengths, fewer than two elements, or a value is non-finite. InvalidOperationException Thrown when either sequence has zero variance. Covariance<T>(IEnumerable<T>, IEnumerable<T>) Returns the sample covariance between two sequences of equal length. public static T Covariance<T>(IEnumerable<T> x, IEnumerable<T> y) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T> Parameters x IEnumerable<T> First sequence, all values finite. y IEnumerable<T> Second sequence, all values finite. Returns T The sample covariance. Type Parameters T Floating-point element type. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when sequences have different lengths, fewer than two elements, or a value is non-finite. Mean<T>(IEnumerable<T>) Returns the arithmetic mean of the sequence. Uses Welford's online algorithm for numerical stability. public static T Mean<T>(IEnumerable<T> values) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T> Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Input sequence. Must contain at least one element, all finite. Returns T The mean value. Type Parameters T Floating-point element type. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when values is empty or contains a non-finite value. Median<T>(IEnumerable<T>) Returns the median of the sequence. For an even number of elements, returns the lower of the two middle values. public static T Median<T>(IEnumerable<T> values) where T : struct, IComparable<T> Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Input sequence. Must contain at least one element. Returns T The median value. Type Parameters T Comparable element type. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when values is empty. StdDev<T>(IEnumerable<T>) Returns the sample standard deviation of the sequence. public static T StdDev<T>(IEnumerable<T> values) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T>, IRootFunctions<T> Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Input sequence. Must contain at least two elements. Returns T The standard deviation. Type Parameters T Floating-point element type. Variance<T>(IEnumerable<T>) Returns the sample variance (divided by n−1) of the sequence. Uses Welford's online algorithm for numerical stability. public static T Variance<T>(IEnumerable<T> values) where T : struct, IFloatingPoint<T> Parameters values IEnumerable<T> Input sequence. Must contain at least two elements, all finite. Returns T The sample variance. Type Parameters T Floating-point element type. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when fewer than two elements are provided, or a value is non-finite.

api/Utils.Mathematics.html.title: Namespace Utils.Mathematics | omy.Utils Documentation
api/Utils.Mathematics.html.summary: Namespace Utils.Mathematics Classes FloatingPointComparer<T> Provides floating-point comparisons that allow for small deviations using a configurable interval. Implements only IComparer<T>: fuzzy equality cannot be used for hashing. MathEx Provides extended mathematical functions and utilities, including custom rounding, clamping, and Pascal's triangle generation. NullableIntEx Provides helper methods for working with nullable numeric endpoints that can represent open ranges by treating null as positive or negative infinity as needed. NumericalMethods Numerical integration and interpolation utilities. Polynomial<T> Represents a polynomial with coefficients of type T. Coefficients are stored as [a₀, a₁, …, aₙ] representing a₀ + a₁x + … + aₙxⁿ. Statistics Descriptive statistics computed over sequences of floating-point values. Trigonometry<T> Provides a base implementation of IAngleCalculator<T> for custom angle measures such as Degrees or Grades, along with default trigonometric/hyperbolic implementations. Interfaces IAngleCalculator<T> Provides an interface for angle-based calculations in various units (Radians, Degrees, Grades). Includes standard and extended trigonometric, hyperbolic, and inverse functions.

Safe to call from within a command handler. public void CloseAfterResponse() Remarks The flag is checked in ProcessQueueAsync immediately after ALL response lines for the current command have been flushed to the stream. This guarantees the final response is received by the peer before the TCP session is torn down. Handlers must NOT call Dispose() or cancel the listen token directly: those would race with ProcessQueueAsync's write lock and risk dropping the in-flight response before it reaches the client. It then generates expression-based writers that serialize each DNS element's fields into the DNS wire format. When writing a DNS packet: The DNS header is written first, updating record counts (QDCount, ANCount, etc.). Each request record is written, translating its string Type to a numeric code (requestClassTypes). Each response record is written, including the user-defined RData portion. The final output is a byte[] representing the DNS packet to be sent over the network or stored. Constructors DNSCanonicalWriter(IEnumerable<DNSFactory>) Initializes a new instance of the DNSCanonicalWriter class, scanning the given DNSFactory collections for DNS record detail types. public DNSCanonicalWriter(IEnumerable<DNSFactory> factories) Parameters factories IEnumerable<DNSFactory> A collection of DNSFactory instances to load DNS types from. Properties Default Gets the default DNSCanonicalWriter instance, which scans the DNS types from Default. public static DNSCanonicalWriter Default { get; } Property Value DNSCanonicalWriter Methods Write(DNSHeader) Serializes the provided DNS header into the target transport representation. public byte[] Write(DNSHeader header) Parameters header DNSHeader The DNS header to serialize. Returns byte[] The serialized output. Write(DNSResponseRecord) Converts the provided DNS response record into its canonical on-the-wire representation. public byte[] Write(DNSResponseRecord record) Parameters record DNSResponseRecord The DNS response record to serialize. Returns byte[] The trimmed byte buffer that contains the canonical DNS payload.

api/Utils.Net.DNS.DNSConstants.html.summary: Class DNSConstants Namespace Utils.Net.DNS Assembly Utils.Net.dll Constants representing DNS datagram flags and fields masks. public static class DNSConstants Inheritance object DNSConstants Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The bit layout within the DNS header defined in RFC 1035 §4.1.1 is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | ID | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ |QR| Opcode |AA|TC|RD|RA| Z|AD|CD| RCODE | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | QDCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | ANCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | NSCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | ARCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ Each mask below targets the corresponding field within this layout. Fields AuthenticDatas indicates in a response that all the data included in the answer and authority portion of the response has been authenticated by the server according to the policies of that server. public const ushort AuthenticDatas = 32 Field Value ushort AuthoritativeAnswer Authoritative Answer bit. Set to 1 if the responding server is an authority for the queried domain. public const ushort AuthoritativeAnswer = 1024 Field Value ushort CheckingDisabled indicates in a query that Pending(non-authenticated) data is acceptable to the resolver sending the query. public const ushort CheckingDisabled = 16 Field Value ushort Error Error field. Indicates the type of DNS error encountered in the response. public const ushort Error = 15 Field Value ushort See Also DNSError MessageTruncated Message Truncated bit. Set to 1 if the DNS message was truncated during transmission. public const ushort MessageTruncated = 512 Field Value ushort OpCode OpCode (Operation Code) field. Indicates the type of DNS query. public const ushort OpCode = 30720 Field Value ushort See Also DNSOpCode QR QR (Query/Response) bit. Set to 1 for Response, 0 for Query. public const ushort QR = 32768 Field Value ushort See Also DNSQRBit RecursionDesired Recursion Desired bit. Set to 1 if the client requests a recursive DNS query. public const ushort RecursionDesired = 256 Field Value ushort RecursionPossible Recursion Possible bit. Set to 1 if the server supports recursive queries. public const ushort RecursionPossible = 8 Field Value ushort ReservedZ Reserved Zero (Z) bits. Reserved for future use. Should be set to 0. public const ushort ReservedZ = 64 Field Value ushort

NameError = 3 Name Error - Meaningful only for responses from an authoritative name server, this code signifies that the domain name referenced in the query does not exist. NotImplemented = 4 Not Implemented - The name server does not support the requested kind of query. Ok = 0 No error condition Refused = 5 Refused - The name server refuses to perform the specified operation for policy reasons. For example, a name server may not wish to provide the information to the particular requester, or a name server may not wish to perform a particular operation (e.g., zone transfer) for particular data. ServerFailure = 2 Server failure - The name server was unable to process this query due to a problem with the name server.

api/Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeader.html.summary: Class DNSHeader Namespace Utils.Net.DNS Assembly Utils.Net.dll Represents the DNS header section of a DNS packet, including standard bits from RFC 1035 §4.1.1 and the AD/CD extensions introduced in RFC 2535. The header provides information about flags, counts for queries/answers/authorities/additionals, and other DNS-level metadata. public class DNSHeader : DNSElement Inheritance object DNSElement DNSHeader Inherited Members DNSElement.DNSType object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) MathEx.Clamp<T>(T, T, T, IComparer<T>) FluentExtensions.Failure<T>(T) FluentExtensions.IsEqualTo<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsGreaterOrEqualsThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsGreaterThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsIn<T>(T, params T[]) FluentExtensions.IsLowerOrEqualsThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsLowerThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsNotIn<T>(T, params T[]) FluentExtensions.IsNotNull<T>(T) FluentExtensions.IsNull<T>(T) FluentExtensions.Success<T>(T) FluentExtensions.Test<T>(T, Func<T, bool>) ObjectUtils.Between<T>(T, IComparer<T>, T, T) ObjectUtils.Between<T>(T, IComparer<T>, T, T, bool, bool) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Task<Result>>, Func<Task<Result>>) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Task<Result>>, Result) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Func<Result>) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Result) ObjectUtils.Do<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Func<Result>) ObjectUtils.Do<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Result) ObjectUtils.In<T>(T, params T[]) ObjectUtils.NotIn<T>(T, params T[]) Validations.ArgMustBe<T>(T, Func<T, bool>, string, string) Validations.ArgMustNotBe<T>(T, Func<T, bool>, string, string) Validations.Arg<T>(T, string) Validations.ValueMustNotBeNull<T>(T, string) Validations.Variable<T>(T, string) Remarks A DNS header contains: ID: A 16-bit identifier assigned by the program that generates any kind of DNS query. Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeader.Flags: A collection of DNS flags and codes packed into a 16-bit field (e.g., QR, OpCode, AA, etc.). Counts of queries, answers, authority records, and additional records (see Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeader.QDCount, Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeader.ANCount, Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeader.NSCount, Utils.Net.DNS.DNSHeader.ARCount). The wire format defined in RFC 1035 §4.1.1 (extended by RFC 2535) is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | ID | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ |QR| Opcode |AA|TC|RD|RA| Z|AD|CD| RCODE | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | QDCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | ANCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | NSCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | ARCOUNT | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ RFC 2535 introduces the AD (Authenticated Data) and CD (Checking Disabled) bits. Clients should only trust AD when the upstream server is trusted, and the CD flag allows requesting unsigned data for local validation. Constructors DNSHeader() Initializes a new instance of the DNSHeader class with a randomly generated ID and default QR bit set to Question. public DNSHeader() Properties Additionals Gets a list of DNS additional records associated with this header. public IList<DNSResponseRecord> Additionals { get; } Property Value IList<DNSResponseRecord> AuthenticDatas Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Authentic Data (AD) bit is set. public bool AuthenticDatas { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks This bit (introduced in RFC 2535) indicates that all data included in the answer and authority sections of the response has been authenticated by the server according to the server's policies. AuthoritativeAnswer Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Authoritative Answer (AA) bit is set. public bool AuthoritativeAnswer { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks This bit is set in responses when the responding server is an authority for the domain in question. Authorities Gets a list of DNS authority records associated with this header. public IList<DNSResponseRecord> Authorities { get; } Property Value IList<DNSResponseRecord> CheckingDisabled Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Checking Disabled (CD) bit is set. public bool CheckingDisabled { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks This bit (introduced in RFC 2535) is set in a query to indicate that Pending (non-authenticated) data is acceptable to the resolver sending the query. ErrorCode Gets or sets the error code (RCODE) in the DNS header. public DNSError ErrorCode { get; set; } Property Value DNSError Remarks This indicates the status of the response (e.g., FormatError, ServerFailure, etc.). ID Gets or sets the 16-bit DNS packet identifier. [DNSField] public ushort ID { get; set; } Property Value ushort MessageTruncated Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Message Truncated (TC) bit is set. public bool MessageTruncated { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks If true, the total size of the DNS packet exceeded the allowable size, and the message was truncated. OpCode Gets or sets the DNSOpCode portion of the DNS flags, indicating the requested operation (e.g., Standard query, Inverse, Status). public DNSOpCode OpCode { get; set; } Property Value DNSOpCode QrBit Gets or sets the DNSQRBit (query or response) portion of the DNS flags. public DNSQRBit QrBit { get; set; } Property Value DNSQRBit RecursionDesired Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Recursion Desired (RD) bit is set. public bool RecursionDesired { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks In a query, setting this bit requests that the DNS server perform recursive resolution on behalf of the client. RecursionPossible Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Recursion Available (RA) bit is set. public bool RecursionPossible { get; set; } Property Value bool Remarks In a response, if this bit is set, the DNS server supports recursive queries. Requests Gets a list of DNS question records associated with this header. public IList<DNSRequestRecord> Requests { get; } Property Value IList<DNSRequestRecord> ReservedFlags Gets or sets the portion of the header reserved for future use (Z bits). public byte ReservedFlags { get; set; } Property Value byte Remarks Historically, these bits must be zero, but with RFC 2535, some of them were repurposed for AD/CD. Responses Gets a list of DNS answer records associated with this header. public IList<DNSResponseRecord> Responses { get; } Property Value IList<DNSResponseRecord> Methods Append(DNSHeader) Merges (appends) the requests, responses, authority, and additional records from another DNSHeader into the current one, provided the IDs do not match. public void Append(DNSHeader header) Parameters header DNSHeader Another DNSHeader to merge from. Exceptions Exception Thrown if the header has the same ID as the current header. ToString() Returns a string describing the current header fields and associated request/response sets. public override string ToString() Returns string A human-readable string representation of this DNS header.

api/Utils.Net.DNS.DNSPacketWriter.html.summary: Class DNSPacketWriter Namespace Utils.Net.DNS Assembly Utils.Net.dll Provides functionality to serialize DNS packets into a byte array using reflection and expression trees. This class can handle standard DNS headers, request records, response records, and user-defined DNS response details annotated with DNSRecordAttribute. public class DNSPacketWriter : IDNSWriter<byte[]> Inheritance object DNSPacketWriter Implements IDNSWriter<byte[]> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) It then generates expression-based writers that serialize each DNS element's fields into the DNS wire format. When writing a DNS packet: The DNS header is written first, updating record counts (QDCount, ANCount, etc.). Each request record is written, translating its string Type to a numeric code (requestClassTypes). Each response record is written, including the user-defined RData portion. The final output is a byte[] representing the DNS packet to be sent over the network or stored. Constructors DNSPacketWriter(IEnumerable<DNSFactory>) Initializes a new instance of the DNSPacketWriter class, scanning the given DNSFactory collections for DNS record detail types. public DNSPacketWriter(IEnumerable<DNSFactory> factories) Parameters factories IEnumerable<DNSFactory> A collection of DNSFactory instances to load DNS types from. DNSPacketWriter(params DNSFactory[]) Initializes a new instance of the DNSPacketWriter class, combining the given DNSFactory instances into one writer. public DNSPacketWriter(params DNSFactory[] factories) Parameters factories DNSFactory[] One or more DNSFactory instances to load DNS types from. Properties Default Gets the default DNSPacketWriter instance, which scans the DNS types from Default. public static DNSPacketWriter Default { get; } Property Value DNSPacketWriter Methods Write(DNSHeader) Serializes the provided DNS header into the target transport representation. public byte[] Write(DNSHeader header) Parameters header DNSHeader The DNS header to serialize. Returns byte[] The serialized output.

Utils.Net.DNS.DNSRequestRecord.RequestType: A 16-bit identifier for the query type (e.g., 0x01 for A records). Class: The DNS class, typically IN or ALL. It also provides an explicit Type property representing the record type in string format. The Clone() method returns a shallow copy of this record’s essential fields. The query section wire layout from RFC 1035 §4.1.2 is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / NAME / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | TYPE | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | CLASS | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ Constructors DNSRequestRecord() Initializes a new instance of the DNSRequestRecord class. public DNSRequestRecord() DNSRequestRecord(string, string, DNSClassId) Initializes a new instance of the DNSRequestRecord class with the specified type, name, and optionally, a DNS class. public DNSRequestRecord(string type, string name, DNSClassId @class = DNSClassId.ALL) Parameters type string The string identifier for the requested record type (e.g., "A", "AAAA"). name string The domain name being queried. class DNSClassId The DNS class for the query (default is ALL). Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when type or name is null. Properties Class Gets or sets the DNS class of the request (e.g., IN or ALL). [DNSField] public DNSClassId Class { get; set; } Property Value DNSClassId Name Gets or sets the domain name being queried. [DNSField] public DNSDomainName Name { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName Type Gets or sets the string identifier for the requested DNS record type (e.g., "A", "AAAA", "MX"). public string Type { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks This property is not directly part of the DNS wire format; rather, it determines Utils.Net.DNS.DNSRequestRecord.RequestType internally when the record is serialized. Methods Clone() Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance. public object Clone() Returns object A new object that is a copy of this instance. ToString() Returns a string that represents the current object. public override string ToString() Returns string A string that represents the current object.

The numeric Class of the response (e.g., 1 for A, 28 for AAAA), stored in Utils.Net.DNS.DNSResponseDetail.ClassId. The DNS class (ClassId), typically IN. The TTL, or time-to-live, indicating how long the record may be cached. An RDLength (2 bytes) indicating the size of the resource data. The RData, which is a DNSResponseDetail object representing the record-specific data (e.g., IP addresses, mail server, etc.). The wire layout in RFC 1035 §4.1.3 is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / NAME / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | TYPE | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | CLASS | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | TTL | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | RDLENGTH | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--| / RDATA / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ Constructors DNSResponseRecord() Initializes a new instance of the DNSResponseRecord class. public DNSResponseRecord() DNSResponseRecord(string, uint, DNSResponseDetail) Initializes a new instance of the DNSResponseRecord class with the specified domain name, TTL, and record details. public DNSResponseRecord(string name, uint TTL, DNSResponseDetail details) Parameters name string The domain name to which the record pertains. TTL uint The time-to-live for this record. details DNSResponseDetail A concrete DNSResponseDetail subclass representing the record data. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if name or details is null. Properties Class Gets or sets the numeric record type for this DNS response. Typically set by RData's Utils.Net.DNS.DNSResponseDetail.ClassId. [DNSField] public ushort Class { get; } Property Value ushort ClassId Gets or sets the DNS class for this response (commonly IN). [DNSField] public DNSClassId ClassId { get; } Property Value DNSClassId Name Gets or sets the DNS domain name associated with this response record. [DNSField] public DNSDomainName Name { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName RDLength Gets or sets the length of the RData section, in bytes. This is determined during serialization. [DNSField] public ushort RDLength { get; } Property Value ushort RData Gets or sets the DNSResponseDetail describing the record data (RDATA). Setting this value updates Class to reflect the RData's Utils.Net.DNS.DNSResponseDetail.ClassId. public DNSResponseDetail RData { get; set; } Property Value DNSResponseDetail TTL Gets or sets the time-to-live (TTL) for this record, indicating how long it may be cached. [DNSField] public uint TTL { get; set; } Property Value uint Methods Clone() Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance. public object Clone() Returns object A new object that is a copy of this instance. Remarks The returned object is an anonymous type containing copies of the basic fields. The RData is also cloned, calling Clone() to duplicate its content fields. ToString() Returns a multi-line string describing this response record, including its domain name, class, TTL, and the specific RData fields. public override string ToString() Returns string A human-readable summary of this DNS response record.

If the address family is unsupported, a NotSupportedException is thrown. Internally, this class overrides Utils.Net.DNS.DNSResponseDetail.ClassId and Name to ensure the wire-format ID matches the address type. Properties IPAddress Gets or sets the IPAddress associated with this record. If the address family is not IPv4, IPv6, or Osi, a NotSupportedException is thrown. public IPAddress IPAddress { get; set; } Property Value IPAddress Exceptions NotSupportedException Thrown when an address family outside of InterNetwork, InterNetworkV6, or Osi is assigned. Name Gets the human-readable DNS record name from the DNSRecordAttribute or defaults to the class name if none is specified. public override string Name { get; } Property Value string Exceptions NotSupportedException Thrown if the IP address family is not InterNetwork, InterNetworkV6, or Osi. Methods ToString() Returns the string representation of the underlying IPAddress. public override string ToString() Returns string The IP address in dotted-decimal or colon-hex notation.

For example, if "www.example.com" is a CNAME to "example.com", then "www.example.com" is the alias, and "example.com" is the canonical name. CNAME records do not directly trigger additional section processing in standard DNS resolution. However, a DNS server may internally restart the resolution at the canonical name, as described in RFC 1034 §3.6.2. This class corresponds to a DNS record with type code 0x05 and DNS class IN. The RDATA layout is simply the canonical domain name: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / CNAME / / / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ The CNAME value is a <domain-name> specifying the canonical target. Properties CName Gets or sets the canonical domain name to which the owner name is aliased. [DNSField] public DNSDomainName CName { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName Methods ToString() Returns the canonical (target) name of this CNAME record. public override string ToString() Returns string The string representation of CName, or an empty string if CName is null.

Traditionally, this is stored as two separate character strings (CPU and OS). However, this example consolidates both into a single Info field. If you prefer to store CPU and OS separately, you can replace Info with two string fields annotated with DNSFieldAttribute. Example (traditional two-field approach): [DNSField] public string CPU { get; set; } [DNSField] public string OS { get; set; } The wire-format RDATA (RFC 1035 §3.3.2) is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / CPU / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / OS / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ Each field is a <character-string>. CPU describes the processor type, and OS identifies the operating system. See RFC 1010 for common values. Properties Info Gets or sets a string containing CPU and OS information. This example uses a single field, but you may split it into two fields if desired (CPU and OS). [DNSField] public string Info { get; set; } Property Value string Methods ToString() Returns the entire stored text in Info. public override string ToString() Returns string

api/Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.MX.html.summary: Class MX Namespace Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035 Assembly Utils.Net.dll Represents an MX (Mail Exchange) record in the DNS, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.9 and further clarified by RFC 974. The MX record specifies a mail server responsible for accepting email on behalf of a domain, along with a preference value. [DNSRecord(DNSClassId.IN, 15, null)] [DNSTextRecord("{Preference} {Exchange}")] public class MX : DNSResponseDetail, ICloneable Inheritance object DNSElement DNSResponseDetail MX Implements ICloneable Inherited Members DNSResponseDetail.Name DNSResponseDetail.Clone() DNSElement.DNSType object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) Lower values are preferred, meaning a mail transfer agent should try servers in ascending order of Preference. Exchange: A domain name specifying the mail exchange host (e.g., mail.example.com). For instance, if example.com has two MX records, one with preference 10 and another with preference 20, the sending mail server will first attempt the host with preference 10. If that fails or is unavailable, it tries the host with preference 20. The DNS library may perform additional processing (e.g., retrieving the A or AAAA address of the Exchange) when resolving MX records. The RDATA layout defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.9 is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | PREFERENCE | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / EXCHANGE / / / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ PREFERENCE is the priority indicator (lower values preferred) and EXCHANGE is a <domain-name> identifying the target mail server. Implementations often follow up with address lookups for the exchange host. Properties Exchange Gets or sets the domain name of the mail exchange server (e.g. mail.example.com). [DNSField] public DNSDomainName Exchange { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName Preference Gets or sets the 16-bit preference value for this MX record. A lower value indicates a higher priority in mail routing. [DNSField] public ushort Preference { get; set; } Property Value ushort Methods ToString() Returns a string representation containing the mail exchange name and its preference, for example: "mail.example.com (10)". public override string ToString() Returns string

api/Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.NS.html.summary: Class NS Namespace Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035 Assembly Utils.Net.dll Represents an NS (Name Server) record as specified in RFC 1035 §3.3.11. An NS record indicates the authoritative name server for a given domain. For example, if this record is under example.com, DNSName might be ns1.example.net, meaning ns1.example.net is an authoritative name server for example.com. Usage typically involves additional section processing within DNS responses to provide "glue records" (A or AAAA RRs) so resolvers can quickly find the IP addresses of these name servers. The wire-format RDATA layout defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.11 is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / NSDNAME / / / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ The NSDNAME value is a <domain-name> that identifies a host which should be authoritative for the owner name. Additional section processing is usually performed to provide glue A or AAAA records for that host. Properties DNSName Gets or sets the domain name of the authoritative name server (e.g., ns1.example.com). [DNSField] public DNSDomainName DNSName { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName

This data may be up to 65535 bytes in length. Since the NULL RR is not intended for standard DNS usage, most resolvers will ignore or discard it unless specifically designed to handle custom data in DNS. Use of this record for production services is discouraged. The on-wire format simply stores arbitrary bytes: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / <anything> / / / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ The payload has no defined semantics; consumers must agree on meaning out-of-band. Properties Datas Gets or sets the raw binary data for this NULL record. May be up to 65535 bytes in length. [DNSField] public byte[] Datas { get; set; } Property Value byte[] Methods ToString() Returns a string consisting of the bytes in hexadecimal form, for example: "{ 54 78 1E 3A ... }". public override string ToString() Returns string

api/Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.PTR.html.summary: Class PTR Namespace Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035 Assembly Utils.Net.dll Represents a PTR (Pointer) record in DNS, as specified by RFC 1035 §3.3.12. [DNSRecord(DNSClassId.IN, 12, null)] [DNSTextRecord("{PTRName}")] public class PTR : DNSResponseDetail, ICloneable Inheritance object DNSElement DNSResponseDetail PTR Implements ICloneable Inherited Members DNSResponseDetail.Name DNSResponseDetail.Clone() DNSElement.DNSType object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) Typically, PTR records are most commonly known for usage in the IN-ADDR.ARPA zone to perform reverse lookups, mapping IP addresses to hostnames. However, a PTR can also appear in other namespaces, simply indicating a pointer to another location in the domain space. Unlike CNAME records, PTRs do not imply special alias or canonical name behavior and cause no additional DNS processing for resolution. They are simply a data pointer. When placed in the IN-ADDR.ARPA domain, the name is usually the reverse-notation IP address (e.g., 1.2.168.192.in-addr.arpa), and the PTRName is the corresponding hostname (e.g., host.example.com). The RDATA format is the target domain name: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / PTRDNAME / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ PTRDNAME is a <domain-name> indicating the pointer destination. Properties PTRName Gets or sets the domain name to which this PTR record points. [DNSField] public DNSDomainName PTRName { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName Remarks For example, in a reverse DNS entry, PTRName would hold the canonical hostname associated with the IP's reverse-lookup domain. Methods ToString() Returns the string representation of PTRName. Because DNSDomainName is a struct, it will never be null, but may be an empty or default value if unset. public override string ToString() Returns string

api/Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.SOA.html.summary: Class SOA Namespace Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035 Assembly Utils.Net.dll Represents an SOA (Start of Authority) record, which defines zone-wide parameters including the primary name server, mailbox of the zone administrator, and various time intervals affecting caching and zone transfers, as described in RFC 1035 §3.3.13. [DNSRecord(DNSClassId.IN, 6, null)] [DNSTextRecord("{MName} {RName} {Serial} {Refresh} {Retry} {Expire} {Minimum}")] public class SOA : DNSResponseDetail, ICloneable Inheritance object DNSElement DNSResponseDetail SOA Implements ICloneable Inherited Members DNSResponseDetail.Name DNSResponseDetail.Clone() DNSElement.DNSType object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) MathEx.Clamp<T>(T, T, T, IComparer<T>) FluentExtensions.Failure<T>(T) FluentExtensions.IsEqualTo<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsGreaterOrEqualsThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsGreaterThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsIn<T>(T, params T[]) FluentExtensions.IsLowerOrEqualsThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsLowerThan<T, CT>(T, CT) FluentExtensions.IsNotIn<T>(T, params T[]) FluentExtensions.IsNotNull<T>(T) FluentExtensions.IsNull<T>(T) FluentExtensions.Success<T>(T) FluentExtensions.Test<T>(T, Func<T, bool>) ObjectUtils.Between<T>(T, IComparer<T>, T, T) ObjectUtils.Between<T>(T, IComparer<T>, T, T, bool, bool) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Task<Result>>, Func<Task<Result>>) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Task<Result>>, Result) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Func<Result>) ObjectUtils.DoAsync<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Result) ObjectUtils.Do<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Func<Result>) ObjectUtils.Do<T, Result>(T, Func<T, Result>, Result) ObjectUtils.In<T>(T, params T[]) ObjectUtils.NotIn<T>(T, params T[]) Validations.ArgMustBe<T>(T, Func<T, bool>, string, string) Validations.ArgMustNotBe<T>(T, Func<T, bool>, string, string) Validations.Arg<T>(T, string) Validations.ValueMustNotBeNull<T>(T, string) Validations.Variable<T>(T, string) Remarks The SOA record (type 0x06) is mandatory at the apex of every DNS zone. It includes: MName: The primary name server for this zone (the origin of zone data). RName: The mailbox of the person responsible for this zone, typically in the form of a domain name, e.g. hostmaster.example.com with a dot replacing the @. Serial: A 32-bit version number for the zone. Secondary name servers use this to detect zone updates. Refresh: The time interval in seconds before secondary name servers should check for updates from the primary. Retry: If a refresh attempt fails, the time (in seconds) to wait before retrying. Expire: The upper limit (in seconds) on how long a secondary may continue serving data if it cannot contact the primary. Minimum: The minimum TTL (in seconds) to be exported with any RR from this zone. Historically also used as the negative-caching TTL in some contexts. In typical zone files, the SOA record looks like: example.com. IN SOA ns1.example.com. hostmaster.example.com. ( 2023031501 ; Serial 10800 ; Refresh (3 hours) 3600 ; Retry (1 hour) 604800 ; Expire (7 days) 3600 ; Minimum (1 hour) ) The RDATA wire format is: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / MNAME / / / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / RNAME / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | SERIAL | | | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | REFRESH | | | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | RETRY | | | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | EXPIRE | | | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ | MINIMUM | | | +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ See RFC 1035 §3.3.13 for authoritative details. Properties Expire Gets or sets the expire value, in seconds, indicating how long a secondary may continue using the zone data if it cannot reach the primary. [DNSField] public uint Expire { get; set; } Property Value uint MName Gets or sets the domain name of the primary master name server for this zone. [DNSField] public DNSDomainName MName { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName Minimum Gets or sets the minimum TTL, in seconds, used as a lower bound for all resource record TTLs in the zone. [DNSField] public uint Minimum { get; set; } Property Value uint RName Gets or sets the mailbox domain name of the person responsible for this zone. Commonly written as something like "hostmaster.example.com" with a '.' in place of '@'. [DNSField] public DNSDomainName RName { get; set; } Property Value DNSDomainName Refresh Gets or sets the refresh interval, in seconds, that a secondary should wait before checking for updates from the primary. [DNSField] public uint Refresh { get; set; } Property Value uint Retry Gets or sets the retry interval, in seconds, for a secondary to wait if the refresh attempt fails. [DNSField] public uint Retry { get; set; } Property Value uint Serial Gets or sets the serial number of this zone. Incrementing this value indicates to secondaries that new data is available. [DNSField] public uint Serial { get; set; } Property Value uint Methods ToString() Returns a multi-line string describing the main parameters of the SOA record. public override string ToString() Returns string

api/Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035.TXT.html.summary: Class TXT Namespace Utils.Net.DNS.RFC1035 Assembly Utils.Net.dll Represents a TXT record in DNS, as defined in RFC 1035 §3.3.14. TXT records store arbitrary human-readable text, often used for descriptive or application-specific data (e.g., SPF, DKIM, etc. in modern usage). [DNSRecord(DNSClassId.IN, 16, null)] [DNSTextRecord("{Text}")] public class TXT : DNSResponseDetail, ICloneable Inheritance object DNSElement DNSResponseDetail TXT Implements ICloneable Inherited Members DNSResponseDetail.Name DNSResponseDetail.Clone() DNSElement.DNSType object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods DataUtils.ToDbValue(object?) In practice, many DNS libraries and servers treat TXT data as either a concatenated string or a list of separate segments. For example: example.com. 3600 IN TXT "Some descriptive text here" The Text property would hold the value "Some descriptive text here". Although RFC 1035 §3.3.14 states "one or more <character-string>s," many modern implementations treat this as a single text block. To conform to multi-segment usage, your library might need additional logic for splitting or concatenating strings if required by a specific environment. The wire format is a sequence of character strings: +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ / TXT-DATA / +--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+--+ Each TXT-DATA chunk begins with a length octet followed by that many bytes of text. Properties Text Gets or sets the text content of this TXT record. In many DNS scenarios, this is either a single string or a concatenation of multiple character-string segments. [DNSField] public string Text { get; set; } Property Value string Methods ToString() Returns the string content of the TXT record. public override string ToString() Returns string
